2026-01-01.celebration: Mary, the Holy Mother of God

quote.text: The Christian mysteries are an indivisible whole. The way from Bethlehem leads inevitably to Golgotha, from the crib to the Cross. The star of Bethlehem shines in the night of sin. The shadow of the Cross falls on the light that shines from the crib. This light is extinguished in the darkness of Good Friday, but it rises all the more brilliantly in the sun of grace on the morning of the Resurrection.
quote.author: St. Edith Stein (Teresa Benedicta of the Cross)

2026-01-02.celebration: Sts. Basil the Great and Gregory Nazianzen, Doctors

quote.text: Quiet, then, as I have said, is the first step in our sanctification; the tongue purified from the gossip of the world; the eyes unexcited by fair color or comely shape; the ear not relaxing the tone of mind by voluptuous songs, nor by that special mischief, the talk of light men and jesters.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

quote.text: Truth is always a quarry hard to hunt, and therefore we must look everywhere for its tracks.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

quote.text: Believe me, dear child, devotion is the sweetest of sweets, the queen of virtues, the perfection of love. If love is the milk of life, devotion is the cream thereof; if it is a fruitful plant, devotion is the blossom; if it is a precious stone, devotion is its brightness; if it is a precious balm, devotion is its perfume, even that sweet odour which delights men and causes the angels to rejoice.
quote.author: St. Francis de Sales

quote.text: For we ought to think of God even more often than we draw our breath; and if the expression is permissible, we ought to do nothing else.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

quote.text: And the Word was made flesh and tabernacled in us (and we saw His glory, the glory as of the Only-Begotten of the Father) full of grace and truth.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria

quote.text: I know, O Lord God Almighty, that I owe You, as the chief duty of my life, the devotion of all my words and thoughts to Yourself.
quote.author: St. Hilary of Poitiers

quote.text: Now the virginity of Mary was hidden from the prince of this world, as was also her offspring, and the death of the Lord; three mysteries of renown, which were wrought in silence by God. How, then, was He manifested to the world? A star shone forth in heaven above all the other stars, the light of which was inexpressible, while its novelty struck men with astonishment.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

quote.text: To-day Godhead sealed itself upon Manhood, that so with the Godhead's stamp Manhood might be adorned.
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

quote.text: Nothing was so necessary for raising our hope as to show us how deeply God loved us. And what could afford us a stronger proof of this than that the Son of God should become a partner with us of human nature?
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

quote.text: The men who have gone beyond this, giving themselves up to God in the dying spiritual way and by renouncing all things, attaining to a state above the contemplation of the forms and images of the mind—in such men, let me assure you, children, God finds His place of rest; there He dwells and there He works as He wills.
quote.author: Johannes Tauler

2026-01-12.seasonLabel: Monday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: But be meek, since the meek shall inherit the earth. Be long-suffering and pitiful and guileless and gentle and good and always trembling at the words which you have heard. You shall not exalt yourself, nor give over-confidence to your soul. Your soul shall not be joined with lofty ones, but with just and lowly ones shall it have its intercourse. The workings that befall you receive as good, knowing that apart from God nothing comes to pass.
quote.author: The Didache

2026-01-13.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Faith and reason are like two wings on which the human spirit rises to the contemplation of truth; and God has placed in the human heart a desire to know the truth—in a word, to know himself—so that, by knowing and loving God, men and women may also come to the fullness of truth about themselves.
quote.author: St. John Paul II

2026-01-14.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: This Good is celebrated by the sacred theologians, both as beautiful and as Beauty, and as Love, and as Beloved; and all the other Divine Names which beseem the beautifying and highly-favoured comeliness.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2026-01-15.seasonLabel: Thursday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Whenever we cover our brother's sin, God will cover ours; whenever we tell people about our brother's guilt, God will do the same about ours.
quote.author: Abba Poemen

2026-01-16.seasonLabel: Friday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: By the Ark of the Covenant we understand the grace of contemplation. An ark is a secret place for gold and silver; the grace of contemplation lays hold of the treasury of celestial wisdom.
quote.author: Richard of St. Victor

2026-01-17.seasonLabel: Saturday of the First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Let him never cease from prayer who has once begun it, be his life ever so wicked; for prayer is the way to amend it, and without prayer such amendment will be much more difficult.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

quote.text: Virtue is a good quality of the mind, by which we live righteously, of which no one can make bad use, which God works in us, without us.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-01-19.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Virginity is really the whole offering of soul and body to be consumed in the fire of love and changed into the flame of its glory.
quote.author: Caryll Houselander

2026-01-20.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God meant things to be much easier than we have made them. A man has a natural right to food, clothing, and shelter. A certain amount of goods is necessary to lead a good life. A family needs work as well as bread. Property is proper to man. We must keep repeating these things. Eternal life begins now.
quote.author: Servant of God Dorothy Day

2026-01-21.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: She knows that she is but a sojourner on the earth, and that among strangers she naturally finds foes; and more than this, that her origin, her dwelling-place, her hope, her recompense, her honours, are above. One thing, meanwhile, she anxiously desires of earthly rulers — not to be condemned unknown.
quote.author: Tertullian

2026-01-22.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He can no longer have God for his Father, who has not the Church for his mother.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2026-01-23.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Poverty, to those who bear it wisely, is a great possession, a treasure that cannot be taken away; the stoutest of staves; a way of gain that cannot be thwarted; a lodging that is safe from snares.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-01-24.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I believe we shall never learn to know ourselves except by endeavouring to know God.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

quote.text: An understanding of this doctrine is so necessary that without it no one can study metaphysics, and ignorance of it gives rise to many errors in other sciences.
quote.author: Thomas Cajetan (Cardinal de Vio)

2026-01-26.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The greatest glory we can give to God is to do his will in everything.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

2026-01-27.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Neither pray as the hypocrites; but as the Lord commanded in His Gospel, thus pray: Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come. Your will be done, as in heaven, so on earth. Give us today our daily (needful) bread, and forgive us our debt as we also forgive our debtors. And bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one; for Yours is the power and the glory forever. Thrice in the day thus pray.
quote.author: The Didache

2026-01-28.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time
2026-01-28.celebration: St. Thomas Aquinas, Priest and Doctor

quote.text: Final and perfect happiness can consist in nothing else than the vision of the Divine Essence.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-01-29.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Even as this broken bread was scattered over the hills, and was gathered together and became one, so let Your Church be gathered together from the ends of the earth into Your kingdom; for Yours is the glory and the power through Jesus Christ forever.
quote.author: The Didache

2026-01-30.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For progress requires that the subject be enlarged in itself, alteration, that it be transformed into something else. The intelligence, then, the knowledge, the wisdom, as well of individuals as of all, as well of one man as of the whole Church, ought, in the course of ages and centuries, to increase and make much and vigorous progress; but yet only in its own kind; that is to say, in the same doctrine, in the same sense, and in the same meaning.
quote.author: St. Vincent of Lérins

2026-01-31.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: There is nothing more pleasing we can offer God than to say to him: 'Possess thyself of us.'
quote.author: St. Louis de Montfort

quote.text: Whoso calm, serene, sedate, / Sets his foot on haughty fate; / Firm and steadfast, come what will, / Keeps his mien unconquered still; / Him the rage of furious seas, / Tossing high wild menaces, / Nor the flames from smoky forges / That Vesuvius disgorges, / Nor the bolt that from the sky / Smites the tower, can terrify.
quote.author: St. Boethius

2026-02-02.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Jesus is at all times assailed by false witnesses, and, while wickedness remains in the world, is ever exposed to accusation. And yet even now He continues silent before these things, and makes no audible answer, but places His defense in the lives of His genuine disciples, which are a pre-eminent testimony, and one that rises superior to all false witness, and refutes and overthrows all unfounded accusations and charges.
quote.author: Origen of Alexandria

2026-02-03.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I believe no happiness can be found worthy to be compared with that of a soul in Purgatory except that of the saints in Paradise; and day by day this happiness grows as God flows into these souls, more and more as the hindrance to His entrance is consumed.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Genoa

2026-02-04.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I am wont to visit My elect in two ways—by temptation and by consolation.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-02-05.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Nothing is worse than envy and malice, nothing more mischievous than vainglory; it is wont to mar ten thousand good things.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-02-06.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He has reached the citadel of no small intelligence who knows that he cannot penetrate the secrets of God. Then, however, we rightly recognize God, when we deny that we know him perfectly.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

2026-02-07.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Admit that God deserves to be loved very much, yea, boundlessly, because He loved us first, He infinite and we nothing, loved us, miserable sinners, with a love so great and so free.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

quote.text: He who hath greater knowledge, and not the fear of God, is the more severe judge of himself.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-02-09.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: There are two things, if I may speak summarily, which have been divinely handed down to us concerning the Virgin: that She was the Spouse of God and Mother of Christ.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

2026-02-10.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: No greater gift could God have given to men than in making His Word, by which He created all things, their Head, and joining them to Him as His members: that the Son of God might become also the Son of man, one God with the Father, one Man with men; so that when we speak to God in prayer for mercy, we do not separate the Son from Him.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-02-11.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: As water extinguishes fire, so almsgiving quenches sin. Here also it is shown and proved, that as in the layer of saving water the fire of Gehenna is extinguished, so by almsgiving and works of righteousness the flame of sins is subdued.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2026-02-12.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The believer has his stand on the Rock; for this reason he cannot be overthrown by the dashing of the billows.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-02-13.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He who is tormented by the passions of the soul, with the adversary lying in wait, should not therefore believe himself to be alienated from Christ because he suffers such things; but rather let him think that by this he is commendable to God, if, while he suffers these things, he praises God rather, not accuses.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

2026-02-14.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: What is wrong is that we do not ask what is right.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

quote.text: We have been taught, and are convinced, and do believe, that He accepts those only who imitate the excellences which reside in Him, temperance, and justice, and philanthropy, and as many virtues as are peculiar to a God who is called by no proper name. And we have been taught that He in the beginning did of His goodness, for man's sake, create all things out of unformed matter.
quote.author: St. Justin Martyr

2026-02-16.seasonLabel: Monday of the Sixth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The world is on fire. Men try to condemn Christ once again, as it were, for they bring a thousand false witnesses against Him. They would raze His Church to the ground—and are we to waste our time upon things which, if God were to grant them, would perhaps bring one soul less to Heaven?
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

2026-02-17.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Sixth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Mary had the greatest need of Christ as Redeemer, for she would have contracted original sin by reason of her descent unless this had been prevented by the grace of a Mediator. As others needed Christ that through His merit sin already contracted might be remitted to them, she needed even more a Mediator preventing this, that she might not at any time be under debt of contracting it, and that she be preserved from actually contracting it.
quote.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus

quote.text: Behold, assiduous meditation on My Passion makes out of a simple man a master of high knowledge; truly it is a living book in which everything is to be found. How right blessed is that man who has it ever before his eyes and studies it!
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

quote.text: Take therefore first, as an indestructible foundation, the Cross, and build upon it the other articles of the faith.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

quote.text: The life of a monk ought at all times to have about it a Lenten character.
quote.author: St. Benedict of Nursia

quote.text: For that death is destroyed, and that the Cross has become the victory over it, and that it has no more power but is verily dead, this is no small proof, or rather an evident warrant, that it is despised by all Christ's disciples, and that they all take the aggressive against it and no longer fear it; but by the sign of the Cross and by faith in Christ tread it down as dead.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

quote.text: Let us then not be ashamed of the Cross of our Saviour, but rather glory in it.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2026-02-23.seasonLabel: Monday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: Do you fast? Give me proof of it by your works. If you see a poor man, take pity on him.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-02-24.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: O sweet exchange! O unsearchable operation! O benefits surpassing all expectation! That the wickedness of many should be hid in a single righteous One, and that the righteousness of One should justify many transgressors!
quote.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus

2026-02-25.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: When the body therefore fasts from food, let the mind fast from vices, and pass judgment upon all earthly cares and desires according to the law of its King.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2026-02-26.seasonLabel: Thursday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: The sixth, to consider how He suffers all this for my sins, etc.; and what I ought to do and suffer for Him.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

2026-02-27.seasonLabel: Friday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: The whole of our treatment and exertion is concerned with the hidden man of the heart, and our warfare is directed against that adversary and foe within us, who uses ourselves as his weapons against ourselves, and, most fearful of all, hands us over to the death of sin.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2026-02-28.seasonLabel: Saturday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: Whoever is desirous of great reward and everlasting salvation, of high knowledge and deep wisdom, of standing erect in joy and sorrow, of possessing full security against all evil, whoever wishes to drink a draught of Thy bitter Passion, and Thy singular sweetness, must carry Thee at all times, O crucified Jesus, before the eyes of his heart.
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

quote.text: Go and sit in your cell and your cell will teach you everything.
quote.author: Abba Moses

2026-03-02.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: Dost thou not know, dear daughter, that all the sufferings, which the soul endures, or can endure, in this life, are insufficient to punish one smallest fault, because the offence, being done to Me, who am the Infinite Good, calls for an infinite satisfaction?
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2026-03-03.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: More easily through fasting does prayer penetrate heaven.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

2026-03-04.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: So it behoves us, if we would have Christ for a frequent guest, to fill our hearts with faithful meditations on the mercy He showed in dying for us, and on His mighty power in rising again from the dead.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2026-03-05.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: Oh, what a sure path is the way of Thy Passion, along the way of truth, up to the very pinnacle of all perfection.
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

2026-03-06.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: O Lord and Master of my life, give me not the spirit of sloth, of despondency, of ambition, or of idle talking; but bestow on me, Thy servant, the spirit of chastity, of humility, of patience, and of love.
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

2026-03-07.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: As you have entered upon a good and most glorious path, run with reverence the race of godliness.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

quote.text: O sweet Jesus, wound my heart, that tears of penitence and love may be my food night and day, and bring me entirely to thee; that my heart may ever be habitable for thee, and my conversation pleasing and acceptable to thee; and the end of my life so praiseworthy, that after the close of this life, I may deserve to praise thee with all thy Saints forever.
quote.author: St. Bridget of Sweden

2026-03-09.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: Fasting is the soul of prayer, mercy is the lifeblood of fasting. Let no one try to separate them; they cannot be separated. If you have only one of them or not all together, you have nothing. So if you pray, fast; if you fast, show mercy; if you want your petition to be heard, hear the petition of others.
quote.author: St. Peter Chrysologus

2026-03-10.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: If you don't deny yourself you will never be a soul of prayer.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2026-03-11.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: A soul does not benefit as it should from the sacrament of confession if it is not humble. Pride keeps it in darkness. The soul neither knows how, nor is it willing, to probe with precision the depths of its own misery. It puts on a mask and avoids everything that might bring it recovery.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

2026-03-12.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: Offer your soul to God, make him an oblation of your fasting, so that your soul may be a pure offering, a holy sacrifice, a living victim, remaining your own and at the same time made over to God. Whoever fails to give this to God will not be excused, for if you are to give him yourself you are never without the means of giving.
quote.author: St. Peter Chrysologus

2026-03-13.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: He who had wished to demonstrate to us, even in His own flesh, the flesh's infirmity, by the reality of suffering, said, 'Father, remove this Your cup'; and remembering Himself, added, 'save that not my will, but Yours be done.' He was the Will and the Power of the Father: and yet, for the demonstration of the patience which was due, He gave Himself up to the Father's Will.
quote.author: Tertullian

2026-03-14.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: There are three things, my brethren, by which faith stands firm, devotion remains constant, and virtue endures. They are prayer, fasting and mercy. Prayer knocks at the door, fasting obtains, mercy receives. Prayer, mercy and fasting: these three are one, and they give life to each other.
quote.author: St. Peter Chrysologus

quote.text: God was born to take us into Himself, suffered to justify us, and died to avenge us; for our manhood abides for ever in Him, the weakness of our infirmity is united with His strength, and the spiritual powers of iniquity and wickedness are subdued in the triumph of our flesh, since God died through the flesh.
quote.author: St. Hilary of Poitiers

2026-03-16.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: It is only by conforming our own will to the divine one that human beings attain their true height, that they become 'divine'; only by coming out of ourselves, only in the 'yes' to God, is Adam's desire — and the desire of us all — to be completely free.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2026-03-17.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: In the distribution of alms and care of the poor, let our Christian fast-times be fat and abound; and let each bestow on the weak and destitute those dainties which he denies himself.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2026-03-18.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: For if we too live as though dying daily, we shall not sin.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

2026-03-19.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week of Lent
2026-03-19.celebration: St. Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary

quote.text: From this love-o'erflowing Passion, I take what fully makes up for the insufficiency of my own small merits; herein lies my complete justification; to meditate on this Passion, I call eternal wisdom, the perfection of all knowledge, the riches of all salvation, an entire satisfaction of all desert; it casts me down in prosperity, it raises me up in adversity, it keeps me in an even balance between this world's weal and woe, and guards me against all evil in complete security.
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

2026-03-20.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: Turn thee with thy whole heart unto the Lord, and forsake this wretched world, and thy soul shall find rest. Learn to despise outward things and to give thyself to things inward, and thou shalt see the kingdom of God come within thee.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-03-21.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: Take my advice, my friend, and be slow to do evil, but swift to your salvation; for readiness to evil and tardiness to good are equally bad.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

quote.text: Strive always to prefer, not that which is easiest, but that which is most difficult; Not that which is most delectable, but that which is most unpleasing; Not that which gives most pleasure, but rather that which gives least; Not that which is restful, but that which is wearisome.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2026-03-23.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: A man is born natural and physical. In baptism he becomes supernatural and theological.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

2026-03-24.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: For the wilderness is the mother of quiet; it is a calm and a harbor, delivering us from all turmoils.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-03-25.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: We needed an Incarnate God, a God put to death, that we might live.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2026-03-26.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: God, of Thy Goodness, give me Thyself: for Thou art enough to me, and I may nothing ask that is less that may be full worship to Thee; and if I ask anything that is less, ever me wanteth,—but only in Thee I have all.
quote.author: Julian of Norwich

2026-03-27.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: As therefore the Lord did nothing without the Father, being united to Him, neither by Himself nor by the apostles, so neither do anything without the bishop and presbyters. Neither endeavour that anything appear reasonable and proper to yourselves apart; but being come together into the same place, let there be one prayer, one supplication, one mind, one hope, in love and in joy undefiled.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2026-03-28.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: Now let us sing the Eternal Life, from which comes the self-existing Life, and every life; and from which, to all things however partaking of life, is distributed the power to live appropriately to each.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2026-03-29.celebration: Palm Sunday of the Passion of the Lord

quote.text: How blessed is he who reaches the fourth degree of love, wherein one loves himself only in God!
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

quote.text: The Word of God, powerful in all things, and not defective with regard to His own justice, did righteously turn against that apostasy, and redeem from it His own property, not by violent means, as the [apostasy] had obtained dominion over us at the beginning, when it insatiably snatched away what was not its own, but by means of persuasion, as became a God of counsel, who does not use violent means to obtain what He desires.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

quote.text: These things therefore being manifest to us, and since we look into the depths of the divine knowledge, it behooves us to do all things in [their proper] order, which the Lord has commanded us to perform at stated times. He has enjoined offerings [to be presented] and service to be performed [to Him], and that not thoughtlessly or irregularly, but at the appointed times and hours. Where and by whom He desires these things to be done, He Himself has fixed by His own supreme will, in order that all things, being piously done according to His good pleasure, may be acceptable unto Him.
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

quote.text: The spouse of Christ cannot be adulterous; she is uncorrupted and pure. She knows one home; she guards with chaste modesty the sanctity of one couch. She keeps us for God. She appoints the sons whom she has born for the kingdom.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2026-04-02.celebration: Holy Thursday — Mass of the Lord's Supper

quote.text: For the new grace of God has renewed all things from carnal unto spiritual, by superinducing the Gospel, the obliterator of the whole ancient bygone system; in which our Lord Jesus Christ has been approved as the Spirit of God, and the Word of God, and the Reason of God: the Spirit, by which He was mighty; the Word, by which He taught; the Reason, by which He came.
quote.author: Tertullian

2026-04-03.celebration: Good Friday of the Passion of the Lord

quote.text: Love, therefore, is the foremost of all excellent achievements and the first of the commandments of the law.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa

quote.text: Worship a little in words, but more by your actions, and rather by keeping the Law than by admiring the Lawgiver; show your love for Him by fleeing from wickedness, pursuing after virtue, living in the Spirit, walking in the Spirit, drawing your knowledge from Him.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2026-04-05.celebration: Easter Sunday of the Resurrection of the Lord

quote.text: And so, Lord, do thou, who dost give understanding to faith, give me, so far as thou knowest it to be profitable, to understand that thou art as we believe; and that thou art that which we believe.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-04-06.seasonLabel: Monday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: We are an Easter People and Alleluia is our song!
quote.author: St. John Paul II

2026-04-07.seasonLabel: Tuesday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: It was the first day of a new creation, with a new heaven and a new earth; and in a semblance of the gardener God walked again in the garden, in the cool not of the evening but the dawn.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2026-04-08.seasonLabel: Wednesday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: Hell took a body, and met God face to face. It took earth, and encountered Heaven.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-04-09.seasonLabel: Thursday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: Hence, there is no doubt that there exists a being, than which nothing greater can be conceived, and it exists both in the understanding and in reality.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-04-10.seasonLabel: Friday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: What art thou, except that which, as the highest of all beings, alone exists through itself, and creates all other things from nothing?
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-04-11.seasonLabel: Saturday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: All grace flows from mercy, and the last hour abounds with mercy for us. Let no one doubt concerning the goodness of God; even if a person's sins were as dark as night, God's mercy is stronger than our misery. One thing alone is necessary: that the sinner set ajar the door of his heart, be it ever so little, to let in a ray of God's merciful grace, and then God will do the rest.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

2026-04-12.celebration: Divine Mercy Sunday (Second Sunday of Easter)

quote.text: Yesterday I was crucified with Him; today I am glorified with Him. Yesterday I died with Him; today I am quickened with Him.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2026-04-13.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: Thou hast found him to be a being which is the highest of all beings, a being than which nothing better can be conceived; that this being is life itself, light, wisdom, goodness, eternal blessedness and blessed eternity; and that it is every where and always.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-04-14.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: We say that the Resurrection is the reconstitution of our nature in its original form.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa

2026-04-15.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: I am love and Mercy Itself. There is no misery that could be a match for My mercy, neither will misery exhaust it, because as it is being granted—it increases. The soul that trusts in My mercy is most fortunate, because I Myself take care of it.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

2026-04-16.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: For by the Word of God being made man has come about the destruction of death and the resurrection of life.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

2026-04-17.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: Do not be afraid of Christ! He takes nothing away, and he gives you everything.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2026-04-18.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: The Spirit of Truth Himself makes the house of His glory shine with the brightness of His light, and will have nothing dark nor lukewarm in His temple.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

quote.text: Love the one good in which are all goods, and it sufficeth. Desire the simple good which is every good, and it is enough.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-04-20.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: No virtue, my daughter, can have life in itself except through charity and humility, which is the foster-mother and nurse of charity.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2026-04-21.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: Moreover, in the Catholic Church itself, all possible care must be taken, that we hold that faith which has been believed everywhere, always, by all. For that is truly and in the strictest sense Catholic, which, as the name itself and the reason of the thing declare, comprehends all universally. This rule we shall observe if we follow universality, antiquity, consent.
quote.author: St. Vincent of Lérins

2026-04-22.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: This is a Feast of feasts and a Solemnity of solemnities as far exalted above all others (not only those which are merely human and creep on the ground, but even those which are of Christ Himself, and are celebrated in His honour) as the Sun is above the stars.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2026-04-23.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: This is the Halleluia which we sing, which, as you know, meaneth, Praise ye the Lord. Therefore that period is before the Lord's Resurrection, this, after His Resurrection: by which time is signified the future hope which as yet we have not: for what we represent after the Lord's Resurrection, we shall have after our own.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-04-24.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: Until I enter into thy joy, O Lord, who art the Three and the One God, blessed for ever and ever. Amen.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-04-25.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: For thus we have been enriched by His poverty, the human nature having been brought up in Him to a God-befitting dignity, and established on thrones beyond all things.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria

quote.text: I do not seek to understand that I may believe, but I believe in order to understand.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2026-04-27.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: The whole point of St. Francis of Assisi is that he certainly was ascetical and he certainly was not gloomy.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2026-04-28.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: I will spend my Heaven in doing good upon earth.
quote.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

2026-04-29.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week of Easter
2026-04-29.celebration: St. Catherine of Siena, Virgin and Doctor

quote.text: The soul, who is lifted by a very great and yearning desire for the honour of God and the salvation of souls, begins by exercising herself, for a certain space of time, in the ordinary virtues, remaining in the cell of self-knowledge, in order to know better the goodness of God towards her.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2026-04-30.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: God has created all things for good; all things for their greatest good; everything for its own good.
quote.author: St. John Henry Newman

2026-05-01.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: For He was made man that we might be made God.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

2026-05-02.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: For God is good, or rather is essentially the source of goodness: nor could one that is good be niggardly of anything: whence, grudging existence to none, He has made all things out of nothing by His own Word, Jesus Christ our Lord.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

quote.text: Believe me, my daughter, there is no way to God save through this door.
quote.author: St. Francis de Sales

2026-05-04.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: This, I say, is the end of all perfection—that the soul may become so purified from every earthly longing, and so raised to spiritual things, that at last the whole life and the desires of the heart form one unbroken prayer.
quote.author: St. Albert the Great

2026-05-05.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: There we shall rest and see, see and love, love and praise. This is what shall be in the end without end.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-05-06.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: From all, learn gladly what you do not know, because humility can make common to you what nature has made proper to each. You will be wiser than all, if you are willing to learn from all.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

2026-05-07.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: The study of inspired Scripture is the chief way of finding our duty, for in it we find both instruction about conduct and the lives of blessed men, delivered in writing, as some breathing images of godly living, for the imitation of their good works.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

2026-05-08.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: Whoever believes that Aristotle was a god, must also believe that he never erred. But if one believe that Aristotle was a man, then doubtless he was liable to error just as we are.
quote.author: St. Albert the Great

2026-05-09.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: They have given the preference to the ascent through negations, as lifting the soul out of things kindred to itself, and conducting it through all the Divine conceptions, above which towers that which is above every name, and every expression and knowledge, and at the furthest extremity attaching it to Him, as far indeed as is possible for us to be attached to that Being.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

quote.text: The beginning of discipline is humility: first, that he hold no knowledge, no scripture as vile; second, that he not be ashamed to learn from anyone; third, that when he shall have attained knowledge, he not contemn the others.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

2026-05-11.seasonLabel: Monday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: Praised be my Lord God, with all His creatures, and especially our brother the Sun, who brings us the day, and who brings us the light.
quote.author: St. Francis of Assisi

2026-05-12.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: This is the way, beloved, in which we find our Saviour, even Jesus Christ, the High Priest of all our offerings, the defender and helper of our infirmity. By Him we look up to the heights of heaven. By Him we behold, as in a glass, His immaculate and most excellent visage. By Him are the eyes of our hearts opened. By Him our foolish and darkened understanding blossoms up anew towards His marvellous light.
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

2026-05-13.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: Despise not the grace because it is freely given, but receive and treasure it devoutly.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2026-05-14.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: When we say: Your kingdom come, which shall certainly come whether we wish it or not, we do by these words stir up our own desires for that kingdom, that it may come to us, and that we may be found worthy to reign in it.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-05-15.seasonLabel: Friday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: In the evening of our life, we shall be judged by love, namely, by the sincerity of our love for God, for our own soul, for our neighbor.
quote.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP

2026-05-16.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: Learn all things; afterward you will see that nothing is superfluous. Constricted knowledge is not pleasant.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

quote.text: The common belief of all mankind agrees that God, the supreme of all things, is good. For since nothing can be imagined better than God, how can we doubt Him to be good than whom there is nothing better?
quote.author: St. Boethius

2026-05-18.seasonLabel: Monday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: The cheerfulness you should have is not the kind we might call physiological good spirits — the happiness of a healthy animal. You must seek something more: the supernatural happiness that comes from the abandonment of everything and the abandonment of yourself into the loving arms of our Father-God.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2026-05-19.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: And by thought it is that He creates, and thought is the basis of the work, the Word filling it and the Spirit perfecting it.
quote.author: St. John of Damascus

2026-05-20.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: Now every one will admit, that the 'Art of dying Well' is the most important of all sciences; at least every one who seriously reflects, how after death we shall have to give an account to God of every thing we did, spoke, or thought of, during our whole life, even of every idle word; and that the devil being our accuser, our conscience a witness, and God the Judge, a sentence of happiness or misery everlasting awaits us.
quote.author: St. Robert Bellarmine

2026-05-21.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: Christ is the morning star, who promises and reveals to the saints the eternal light of life, when the night of the world is past.
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

2026-05-22.seasonLabel: Friday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: Don't let your life be barren. Be useful. Make yourself felt. Shine forth with the torch of your faith and your love. With your apostolic life, wipe out the trail of filth and slime left by the corrupt sowers of hatred. And set aflame all the ways of the earth with the fire of Christ that you bear in your heart.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2026-05-23.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: It is a matter of necessity that every Church should agree with this Church, on account of its preeminent authority [potiorem principalitatem].
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

quote.text: It is a greater good to be preserved from evil than to fall into it and afterwards be freed from it. If Christ merited grace and glory for so many souls, who, for these gifts, are indebted to Christ as Mediator, why should no soul be His debtor for the gift of its innocence?
quote.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus

2026-05-25.seasonLabel: Monday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: If you should ask how these things come about, question grace, not instruction; desire, not intellect; the cry of prayer, not pursuit of study; the spouse, not the teacher; God, not man; darkness, not clarity; not light, but the wholly flaming fire which will bear you aloft to God with fullest unction and burning affection.
quote.author: St. Bonaventure

2026-05-26.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Christ is of those who are humble-minded, and not of those who exalt themselves over His flock. Our Lord Jesus Christ, the Sceptre of the majesty of God, did not come in the pomp of pride or arrogance, although He might have done so, but in a lowly condition, as the Holy Spirit had declared regarding Him.
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

2026-05-27.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Christ be with me, Christ within me, Christ behind me, Christ before me, Christ beside me, Christ to win me, Christ to comfort and restore me.
quote.author: St. Patrick (attributed)

2026-05-28.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Thus it is that God, by loving us, restores us to His image, and, in order that He may find in us the form of His goodness, He gives us that whereby we ourselves too may do the work that He does, kindling that is the lamps of our minds, and inflaming us with the fire of His love, that we may love not only Himself, but also whatever He loves.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2026-05-29.seasonLabel: Friday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The glory of a good man is the testimony of a good conscience.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-05-30.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Charity can always grow greater in itself, because it is a participation in uncreated love and unlimited love.
quote.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP

quote.text: For faith is to believe what thou yet seest not; and the reward of faith is, to see what thou believest.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-06-01.seasonLabel: Monday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Rest is in Him alone.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2026-06-02.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Meditation is frequent thinking with counsel, which prudently investigates the cause and origin, the mode and utility of each thing. Meditation takes its beginning from reading, yet is constrained by no rules or precepts of reading.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

2026-06-03.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: By the tabernacle of the covenant we understand the state of perfection. Where perfection of the soul is, there also is the habitation of God.
quote.author: Richard of St. Victor

2026-06-04.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-06-04.celebration: The Most Holy Body and Blood of Christ (Corpus Christi)

quote.text: A person enters into the first tabernacle when he returns to himself. A person enters into the second tabernacle when he goes beyond himself. When going beyond himself surely a person is elevated to God.
quote.author: Richard of St. Victor

2026-06-05.seasonLabel: Friday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God therefore is both Creator and Provider, and His creative and preserving and providing power is simply His good-will.
quote.author: St. John of Damascus

2026-06-06.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The one who said, 'For the temple of God is holy, and you are that temple,' did not lie; likewise when he said: 'You must know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit, who is within you.' Now since we have merited to become the temple of God through His grace and without any preceding merits of our own, dearest brethren, let us put forth as much effort as we can, with His assistance, so that our Lord may not find in His temple, that is, in us, anything to offend the eyes of His majesty. Let the dwelling of our heart be freed of vices and filled with virtues; let it be closed to the devil and open to Christ.
quote.author: St. Caesarius of Arles

quote.text: We are not real social workers. We may be doing social work in the eyes of the people, but we are really contemplatives in the heart of the world.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

2026-06-08.seasonLabel: Monday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love is a great thing, a good above all others, which alone maketh every heavy burden light.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-06-09.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I do not congratulate that believer in Christ whose faith can be shaken by Celsus — who no longer shares the common life of men, but has long since departed — or by any apparent plausibility of argument.
quote.author: Origen of Alexandria

2026-06-10.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: My sins run out behind me, and I do not see them, and today I am coming to judge the errors of another.
quote.author: Abba Moses

2026-06-11.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: All the pleasures of the world, and all the kingdoms of this earth, shall profit me nothing. It is better for me to die on behalf of Jesus Christ, than to reign over all the ends of the earth.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2026-06-12.seasonLabel: Friday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-06-12.celebration: The Most Sacred Heart of Jesus

quote.text: The presence of Christ's true body and blood in this sacrament cannot be detected by sense, nor understanding, but by faith alone, which rests upon Divine authority.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-06-13.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: This is why I said at the beginning that the measure of our love to God is to love immeasurably.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

quote.text: My love has been crucified, and there is no fire in me desiring to be fed; but there is within me a water that lives and speaks, saying to me inwardly, Come to the Father. I have no delight in corruptible food, nor in the pleasures of this life. I desire the bread of God, the heavenly bread, the bread of life, which is the flesh of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2026-06-15.seasonLabel: Monday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: May those, then, who are good, chaste, sober, humble, and gentle, persevere with God's help in their good works; may those who do evil correct themselves at once before their souls depart from this life. If they die without repentance they do not enter into life, but are cast into death.
quote.author: St. Caesarius of Arles

2026-06-16.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Great love can change small things into great ones, and it is only love which lends value to our actions. And the purer our love becomes, the less there will be within us for the flames of suffering to feed upon, and the suffering will cease to be a suffering for us; it will become a delight! By the grace of God, I have received such a disposition of heart that I am never so happy as when I suffer for Jesus, whom I love with every beat of my heart.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

2026-06-17.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Therefore the last and perfect happiness, which we await in the life to come, consists entirely in contemplation.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-06-18.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ.
quote.author: St. Jerome

2026-06-19.seasonLabel: Friday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He who is the Imposer of the burden is Himself the Aider in its execution: and lest the weak recipient should fall beneath the greatness of the grace, He who conferred the dignity will also give the power.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2026-06-20.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For your conduct as a king is based upon the knowledge which by orthodox preaching you have obtained of your God and Creator, whereby you believe and worship Him, and as far as man is able, pay Him the sincere devotion of your mind.
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

quote.text: All true and living devotion presupposes the love of God; and indeed it is neither more nor less than a very real love of God.
quote.author: St. Francis de Sales

2026-06-22.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Who though because of His nature He cannot be attained to, yet because of His goodness can be received by us, filling all things His power, but only partaken of by the just, simple in substance, rich in virtues, present to each, dividing of His own to every one, and Himself whole everywhere.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

2026-06-23.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I think that for those living in community obedience is a greater virtue than chastity, however perfect. Chastity carries within it the danger of pride, but obedience has within it the promise of humility.
quote.author: Amma Syncletica

2026-06-24.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-06-24.celebration: The Nativity of St. John the Baptist

quote.text: In studying nature we have not to inquire how God the Creator may, as He freely wills, use His creatures to work miracles and thereby show forth His power: we have rather to inquire what Nature with its immanent causes can naturally bring to pass.
quote.author: St. Albert the Great

2026-06-25.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Wherefore virtue has need at our hands of willingness alone, since it is in us and is formed from us.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

2026-06-26.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Church, though dispersed throughout the whole world, even to the ends of the earth, has received from the apostles and their disciples this faith: [She believes] in one God, the Father Almighty, Maker of heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all things that are in them; and in one Christ Jesus, the Son of God, who became incarnate for our salvation; and in the Holy Spirit, who proclaimed through the prophets the dispensations of God.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2026-06-27.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Take this love, therefore, as the end that is set before you, to which you are to refer all that you say, and, whatever you narrate, narrate it in such a manner that he to whom you are discoursing on hearing may believe, on believing may hope, on hoping may love.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

quote.text: When I see that the burden is beyond my strength, I do not consider or analyze it or probe into it, but I run like a child to the Heart of Jesus and say only one word to Him: 'You can do all things.' And then I keep silent, because I know that Jesus Himself will intervene in the matter, and as for me, instead of tormenting myself, I use that time to love Him.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

2026-06-29.seasonLabel: Monday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: And more than all other men are we your helpers and allies in promoting peace, seeing that we hold this view, that it is alike impossible for the wicked, the covetous, the conspirator, and for the virtuous, to escape the notice of God, and that each man goes to everlasting punishment or salvation according to the value of his actions.
quote.author: St. Justin Martyr

2026-06-30.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Without faith it is impossible to be saved.
quote.author: St. John of Damascus

2026-07-01.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: O human, who are fragile dust of the earth and ashes of ashes! Cry out and speak of the origin of pure salvation until those people are instructed, who, though they see the inmost contents of the Scriptures, do not wish to tell them or preach them, because they are lukewarm and sluggish in serving God's justice.
quote.author: St. Hildegard of Bingen

2026-07-02.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: There are two ways, one of life and one of death; but a great difference between the two ways. The way of life, then, is this: First, you shall love God who made you; second, your neighbour as yourself; and all things whatsoever you would should not occur to you, do not also do to another.
quote.author: The Didache

2026-07-03.seasonLabel: Friday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He who prays is certainly saved; he who prays not is certainly damned.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

2026-07-04.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Our life is hid with Christ in God. Let, then, no one here strive to shine, let none show pride, let none boast. Christ willed not to be known here, He would not that His Name should be preached in the Gospel while He lived on earth. He came to lie hidden from this world. Let us therefore likewise hide our life after the example of Christ, let us shun boastfulness, let us not desire to be made known. It is better to live here in humility, and there in glory.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

quote.text: Therefore I maintain, my daughters, that we should fix our eyes on Christ our only good, and on His saints; there we shall learn true humility.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

2026-07-06.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Faith is a habit of the mind, whereby eternal life is begun in us, making the intellect assent to what is non-apparent.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-07-07.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It behooves therefore those who have been deemed worthy to see and to hear such things, and who have enjoyed so great a gift, to display also a life worthy of the doctrines, that they may enjoy also the good things which are (laid up) there.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-07-08.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Reputation, after all, is but a signboard giving notice where virtue dwells, and virtue itself is always and everywhere preferable.
quote.author: St. Francis de Sales

2026-07-09.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Happy is our sacrament of water, in that, by washing away the sins of our early blindness, we are set free and admitted into eternal life! But we, little fishes, after the example of our ΙΧΘΥΣ Jesus Christ, are born in water, nor have we safety in any other way than by permanently abiding in water.
quote.author: Tertullian

2026-07-10.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Therefore as long as a thing has being, God must be present to it, according to its mode of being.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-07-11.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Hearken, O my son, to the precepts of thy master, and incline the ear of thine heart.
quote.author: St. Benedict of Nursia

quote.text: Glory to the Hidden One, Whose Son was made manifest! Glory to that Living One, Whose Son was made to die! Glory to that Great One, Whose Son descended and was small!
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

2026-07-13.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Maker of earth and sky, from age to age / Who rul'st the world by reason; at whose word / Time issues from Eternity's abyss.
quote.author: St. Boethius

2026-07-14.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Of all things to be sought, the first is wisdom, in which the form of the perfect good consists.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

2026-07-15.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For ourselves, let us but obey God's voice in our hearts, and I will venture to say we shall have no doubts practically formidable about the truth of Scripture.
quote.author: St. John Henry Newman

2026-07-16.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The whole world cannot afford thee peace, but the peace of God can.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-07-17.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It behoved that there should be sin; but all shall be well, and all shall be well, and all manner of thing shall be well.
quote.author: Julian of Norwich

2026-07-18.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He knew that the praise of God stands on its strongest ground when it stands on nothing.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

quote.text: By no means then is it permitted to speak, or even to think, anything, concerning the superessential and hidden Deity, beyond those things divinely revealed to us in the sacred Oracles.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2026-07-20.seasonLabel: Monday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: How blessed and wonderful, beloved, are the gifts of God! Life in immortality, splendour in righteousness, truth in perfect confidence, faith in assurance, self-control in holiness! And all these fall under the cognizance of our understandings [now]; what then shall those things be which are prepared for such as wait for Him?
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

2026-07-21.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: How I wish your bearing and conversation were such that, on seeing or hearing you, people would say: This man reads the life of Jesus Christ.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2026-07-22.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Our whole life is thus shrouded in mystery. Every one of our salutary acts presupposes the mystery of grace from which it proceeds, and all sin is a mystery of iniquity, presupposing the divine permission to allow evil to exist in view of some higher good purpose, which will be clearly seen only in heaven.
quote.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP

2026-07-23.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The priests indeed are good, but the High Priest is better; to whom the holy of holies has been committed, and who alone has been trusted with the secrets of God. He is the door of the Father, by which enter in Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the prophets, and the apostles, and the Church.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2026-07-24.seasonLabel: Friday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, and Jesus Christ Himself, who is the Son of God, and our everlasting High Priest, build you up in faith and truth, and in all meekness, gentleness, patience, long-suffering, forbearance, and purity; and may He bestow on you a lot and portion among His saints, and on us with you, and on all that are under heaven, who shall believe in our Lord Jesus Christ.
quote.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna

2026-07-25.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: But whatsoever a teacher holds, other than all, or contrary to all, be he holy and learned, be he a bishop, be he a Confessor, be he a martyr, let that be regarded as a private fancy of his own, and be separated from the authority of common, public, general persuasion.
quote.author: St. Vincent of Lérins

quote.text: No indeed, my child, the devotion which is true hinders nothing, but on the contrary it perfects everything; and that which runs counter to the rightful vocation of any one is, you may be sure, a spurious devotion.
quote.author: St. Francis de Sales

2026-07-27.seasonLabel: Monday of the Seventeenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: To be saved, we must know, love and serve God. Oh, what a beautiful life! How good, how great a thing it is to know, to love and serve God! We have nothing else to do in this world. All that we do besides is lost time.
quote.author: St. John Vianney

2026-07-28.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Seventeenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The supreme wisdom is this, to work at the exclusion of all self-seeking, and to be ready to do the will of God.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2026-07-29.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Seventeenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: So then we ought to fear God only, and despise the demons, and be in no fear of them.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

2026-07-30.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Seventeenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The family, which is founded and given life by love, is a community of persons: of husband and wife, of parents and children, of relatives. Its first task is to live with fidelity the reality of communion in a constant effort to develop an authentic community of persons.
quote.author: St. John Paul II

2026-07-31.seasonLabel: Friday of the Seventeenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Man is created to praise, reverence, and serve God our Lord, and by this means to save his soul.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

2026-08-01.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Seventeenth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-08-01.celebration: St. Alphonsus Liguori, Bishop and Doctor

quote.text: God wills only our good; God loves us more than anybody else can or does love us.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

quote.text: I know and I have experienced that 'the Kingdom of God is within us.'
quote.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

2026-08-03.seasonLabel: Monday of the Eighteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: How beautiful is the spectacle to God when a Christian does battle with pain; when he is drawn up against threats, and punishments, and tortures; when, mocking the noise of death, he treads under foot the horror of the executioner; when he raises up his liberty against kings and princes, and yields to God alone, whose he is; when, triumphant and victorious, he tramples upon the very man who has pronounced sentence against him!
quote.author: Minucius Felix

2026-08-04.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Eighteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Prayer is nothing else than union with God.
quote.author: St. John Vianney

2026-08-05.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Eighteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: As often as this life's idle show tries to charm you; as often as you see in the world some vain pomp, transport yourself in mind to Paradise, essay to be now what you will be hereafter.
quote.author: St. Jerome

2026-08-06.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Eighteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love begins at home, and it is not how much we do, but how much love we put in the action that we do.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

2026-08-07.seasonLabel: Friday of the Eighteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It is cause and origin and essence and life of all things; and even of those who fall away from It, both recalling and resurrection; and of those who have lapsed to the perversion of the Divine likeness, renewal and reformation; of those who are tossed about in a sort of irreligious unsteadiness, a religious stability; of those who have continued to stand, steadfastness.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2026-08-08.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Eighteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Though I am poor, Lord, I give thee all I possess; but when I say I give thee my will, I have nothing left to give thee.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

quote.text: The greatest challenge of the day is: how to bring about a revolution of the heart, a revolution which has to start with each one of us?
quote.author: Servant of God Dorothy Day

2026-08-10.seasonLabel: Monday of the Nineteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It is not power, but love that redeems us! This is God's sign: he himself is love.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2026-08-11.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Nineteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For piety is an insatiable thing; and it argues a childish mind to imagine from just the beginnings that you have obtained the whole.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-08-12.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Nineteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: And there is, further, the most Divine Knowledge of Almighty God, which is known, through not knowing (agnosia) during the union above mind; when the mind, having stood apart from all existing things, then having dismissed also itself, has been made one with the super-luminous rays, thence and there being illuminated by the unsearchable depth of wisdom.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2026-08-13.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Nineteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: To the cry of prayer through Christ crucified, by Whose blood we are purged of the filth of vice, do I first invite the reader, lest perchance he should believe that it suffices to read without unction, speculate without devotion, investigate without wonder, examine without exultation, work without piety, know without love, understand without humility, be zealous without divine grace, see without wisdom divinely inspired.
quote.author: St. Bonaventure

2026-08-14.seasonLabel: Friday of the Nineteenth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-08-14.celebration: St. Maximilian Kolbe, Priest and Martyr

quote.text: There is no such thing on earth as an uninteresting subject; the only thing that can exist is an uninterested person.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2026-08-15.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Nineteenth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-08-15.celebration: The Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary

quote.text: All mortal creatures in those anxious aims which find employment in so many varied pursuits, though they take many paths, yet strive to reach one goal—the goal of happiness.
quote.author: St. Boethius

quote.text: The first thing in the study of science is to seek God; then the honesty of life with the work of innocence.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

2026-08-17.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting. It has been found difficult; and left untried.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2026-08-18.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Let us then continually persevere in our hope, and the earnest of our righteousness, which is Jesus Christ, who bore our sins in His own body on the tree, who did no sin, neither was guile found in His mouth, but endured all things for us, that we might live in Him. Let us then be imitators of His patience; and if we suffer for His name's sake, let us glorify Him.
quote.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna

2026-08-19.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The contemplative life is to cling with our whole mind to the love of God and our neighbor, and to desire nothing beside our Creator.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2026-08-20.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: No one there hears Him calling, Come unto me all you that labour. They scorn to learn of Him, because He is meek and lowly of heart; for You have hid those things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-08-21.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: We are speaking of God; what marvel, if thou do not comprehend? For if thou comprehend, He is not God. Be there a pious confession of ignorance, rather than a rash profession of knowledge. To reach to God in any measure by the mind, is a great blessedness; but to comprehend Him, is altogether impossible.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-08-22.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-08-22.celebration: The Queenship of the Blessed Virgin Mary

quote.text: To understand that we have souls, is to feel our separation from things visible, our independence of them, our distinct existence in ourselves, our individuality, our power of acting for ourselves this way or that way, our accountableness for what we do.
quote.author: St. John Henry Newman

quote.text: We who formerly delighted in fornication, but now embrace chastity alone; we who formerly used magical arts, dedicate ourselves to the good and unbegotten God; we who valued above all things the acquisition of wealth and possessions, now bring what we have into a common stock, and communicate to every one in need; we who hated and destroyed one another, and on account of their different manners would not live with men of a different tribe, now, since the coming of Christ, live familiarly with them, and pray for our enemies.
quote.author: St. Justin Martyr

2026-08-24.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I would fain send up a cry so loud that it would put fear in all men on the earth. I would say to them: 'Wretches, why do you let yourselves be thus blinded by the world, you whose need is so great and grievous, as you will know at the moment of death, and who make no provision for it whatsoever?'
quote.author: St. Catherine of Genoa

2026-08-25.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: All things, then, seek the good; indeed, you may express the fact by defining good as that which all desire.
quote.author: St. Boethius

2026-08-26.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Would you do honour to Christ's body? Neglect Him not when naked; do not while here you honour Him with silken garments, neglect Him perishing without of cold and nakedness.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2026-08-27.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I know that angels visit the canonical hours and the congregations of the brethren. What if they do not find me among the brethren? May they not say, 'Where is Bede?'
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

2026-08-28.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: You shall love God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind: so that you are to concentrate all your thoughts, your whole life and your whole intelligence upon Him from whom you derive all that you bring.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-08-29.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twenty-First Week in Ordinary Time
2026-08-29.celebration: The Passion of St. John the Baptist

quote.text: The word of God makes us change our concept of realism: the realist is the one who recognizes in the word of God the foundation of all things.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

quote.text: For to suffer without making excuses is a habit of great perfection, and very edifying and meritorious.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

2026-08-31.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It is necessary, therefore, to purge our minds, in order to be able to see ineffably that which is ineffable; whereto not having yet attained, we are to be nourished by faith, and led by such ways as are more suited to our capacity, that we may be rendered apt and able to comprehend it.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-09-01.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Paul, when enumerating the innumerable causes which generally separate men from the love of Christ and from the love of God in Christ Jesus, to all of which the love that was in himself rose superior, did not set down argument among the grounds of separation. For he says: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword? Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through Him that loved us.
quote.author: Origen of Alexandria

2026-09-02.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For peace is a good so great, that even in this earthly and mortal life there is no word we hear with such pleasure, nothing we desire with such zest, or find to be more thoroughly gratifying.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-09-03.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The one who loves God cannot help but love also every man as himself even though he is displeased by the passions of those who are not yet purified.
quote.author: St. Maximus the Confessor

2026-09-04.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: All waters, therefore, in virtue of the pristine privilege of their origin, do, after invocation of God, attain the sacramental power of sanctification; for the Spirit immediately supervenes from the heavens, and rests over the waters, sanctifying them from Himself.
quote.author: Tertullian

2026-09-05.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twenty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Thus we say that He is worshipped both by ourselves and by the holy angels, not unwisely bringing Him down to a mere humanity, but following the divine Scripture, and binding the Word who was begotten of God to our nature in a union, and weaving the product of both into a certain unity, so that He might not be considered as a mere man who bore God, but rather as God who became man.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria

quote.text: Among the most holy Sacraments of Christ's church the most excellent is the most divine Sacrament of the Eucharist, the Sacrament of Grace, the Sacrament of charity, the Sacrament of love.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

2026-09-07.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Humanity is loved by God! This very simple yet profound proclamation is owed to humanity by the Church. Each Christian's words and life must make this proclamation resound: God loves you, Christ came for you, Christ is for you 'the Way, the Truth and the Life!'
quote.author: St. John Paul II

2026-09-08.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-Third Week in Ordinary Time
2026-09-08.celebration: The Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary

quote.text: Conformity signifies that we join our wills to the will of God. Uniformity means more—it means that we make one will of God's will and ours, so that we will only what God wills; that God's will alone, is our will.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

2026-09-09.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twenty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Before all things, the Teacher of peace and the Master of unity would not have prayer to be made singly and individually, as for one who prays to pray for himself alone. For we say not My Father, which art in heaven, nor Give me this day my daily bread; nor does each one ask that only his own debt should be forgiven him. Our prayer is public and common; and when we pray, we pray not for one, but for the whole people, because we the whole people are one.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2026-09-10.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twenty-Third Week in Ordinary Time
