quote.text: Since men become happy by the acquisition of happiness, while happiness is very Godship, it is manifest that they become happy by the acquisition of Godship.
quote.author: St. Boethius

2026-09-11.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twenty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: As soon as a man seriously seeks truth and goodness, this intimate conversation with himself tends to become conversation with God. Little by little, instead of seeking himself in everything, instead of tending more or less consciously to make himself a center, man tends to seek God in everything, and to substitute for egoism love of God and of souls in Him. This constitutes the interior life.
quote.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP

2026-09-12.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twenty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God is in all things; not, indeed, as part of their essence, nor as an accident, but as an agent is present to that upon which it works.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

quote.text: Action is worth nothing without prayer: prayer grows in value with sacrifice.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2026-09-14.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-09-14.celebration: The Exaltation of the Holy Cross

quote.text: By the simplex and supernatural nature of all beautiful things, all beauty, and everything beautiful, pre-existed uniquely as to Cause.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2026-09-15.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I remained, lost in oblivion; My face I reclined on the Beloved. All ceased and I abandoned myself, Leaving my cares forgotten among the lilies.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2026-09-16.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twenty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The soul in which God dwells by grace is no impersonal scene of the divine life but is itself drawn into this life. The divine life is three-personal life: it is overflowing love, in which the Father generates the Son and gives him his Being, while the Son embraces this Being and returns it to the Father; it is the love in which the Father and Son are one, both breathing the Holy Spirit. By grace this Spirit is shed abroad in men's hearts.
quote.author: St. Edith Stein (Teresa Benedicta of the Cross)

2026-09-17.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twenty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Prayer is an uprising of the mind to God or a petitioning of God for what is fitting.
quote.author: St. John of Damascus

2026-09-18.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twenty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: 'Father,' said I, 'do you see, let us say, this vessel lying here to be a little pitcher, or something else?' And he said, 'I see it to be so.' And I replied to him, 'Can it be called by any other name than what it is?' And he said, 'No.' 'Neither can I call myself anything else than what I am, a Christian.'
quote.author: St. Perpetua

2026-09-19.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twenty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: This gift is with us unto the end of the world, the solace of our waiting, the assurance, by the favours which He bestows, of the hope that shall be ours, the light of our minds, the sun of our souls. This Holy Spirit we must seek and must earn, and then hold fast by faith and obedience to the commands of God.
quote.author: St. Hilary of Poitiers

quote.text: His members might learn to fly from the favours of the world, to be afraid of no terrors, to love adversity for the truth's sake, and to shrink in fear from prosperity; because this often defiles the heart through vain glory, while that purges it through sorrow; in this the mind exalts itself, but in that, even though it had once exalted itself, it brings itself low; in this man forgets himself, but in that, even perforce and against his will, he is recalled to memory of what he is.
quote.author: St. Gregory the Great

2026-09-21.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Holy Spirit is light and strength. He teaches us to distinguish between truth and falsehood, and between good and evil.
quote.author: St. John Vianney

2026-09-22.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The interior life, or the life of the soul with God, well deserves to be called the one thing necessary, since by it we tend to our last end and assure our salvation.
quote.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP

2026-09-23.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twenty-Fifth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-09-23.celebration: St. Pius of Pietrelcina (Padre Pio), Priest

quote.text: COME, then, let us extol the Peace Divine, and Source of conciliation, by hymns of peace! For this it is which unifies all, and engenders, and effects the agreement and fellowship of all.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2026-09-24.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twenty-Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: They abstain from the Eucharist and from prayer, because they confess not the Eucharist to be the flesh of our Saviour Jesus Christ, which suffered for our sins, and which the Father, of His goodness, raised up again.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2026-09-25.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twenty-Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: If thou wouldst know how these things are done, ask grace, not learning; desire, not intellect; the groaning of prayer, not the diligence of reading; the spouse, not the master; God, not man.
quote.author: St. Bonaventure

2026-09-26.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twenty-Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God is the foundation of hope: not any god, but the God who has a human face and who has loved us to the end, each one of us and humanity in its entirety.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

quote.text: I always took delight in learning, or teaching, or writing.
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

2026-09-28.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-Sixth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: We have been taught that God did not make the world aimlessly, but for the sake of the human race; and we have before stated that He takes pleasure in those who imitate His properties, and is displeased with those that embrace what is worthless either in word or deed.
quote.author: St. Justin Martyr

2026-09-29.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-Sixth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-09-29.celebration: Sts. Michael, Gabriel and Raphael, Archangels

quote.text: The will, therefore, is then truly free, when it is not the slave of vices and sins. Such was it given us by God; and this being lost by its own fault, can only be restored by Him who was able at first to give it. And therefore the truth says, If the Son shall make you free, you shall be free indeed.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-09-30.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twenty-Sixth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love finds nothing hard; no task is difficult to the eager. Think of all that Jacob bore for Rachel, the wife who had been promised to him. And they seemed unto him but a few days for the love he had to her.
quote.author: St. Jerome

2026-10-01.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twenty-Sixth Week in Ordinary Time
2026-10-01.celebration: St. Thérèse of the Child Jesus, Virgin and Doctor

quote.text: My God, I choose everything, I will not be a Saint by halves, I am not afraid of suffering for Thee, I only fear one thing, and that is to do my own will. Accept the offering of my will, for I choose all that Thou willest.
quote.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

2026-10-02.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twenty-Sixth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: To attain heaven is to reach our ultimate and supernatural goal, to see God as He sees Himself, to love Him as He loves Himself, to possess Him unfailingly forever.
quote.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP

2026-10-03.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twenty-Sixth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Our God was not satisfied to be among us only by means of the inestimable mystery of the incarnation, by means of which God became man to allow mankind to share in his divine nature, but wanted to go further in an incomprehensible and infallible manner by means of the most divine sacrament of the altar in which truthfully speaking God is with us.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

quote.text: All the virtues assist the mind in the pursuit of Divine Love, but above all does pure prayer.
quote.author: St. Maximus the Confessor

2026-10-05.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: When can the upright man be alone, since he is always with God? When is he left forsaken who is never separated from Christ?
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

2026-10-06.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: O mystery of piety! O sign of unity! O bond of charity!
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-10-07.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twenty-Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Read the divine scriptures constantly; never, indeed, let the sacred volume be out of your hand. Learn what you have to teach. Hold fast the faithful word as you have been taught that you may be able by sound doctrine to exhort and convince the gainsayers.
quote.author: St. Jerome

2026-10-08.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twenty-Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: See that you all follow the bishop, even as Jesus Christ does the Father, and the presbytery as you would the apostles; and reverence the deacons, as being the institution of God. Let no man do anything connected with the Church without the bishop. Wherever the bishop shall appear, there let the multitude [of the people] also be; even as, wherever Jesus Christ is, there is the Catholic Church.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2026-10-09.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twenty-Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: O Thou Good Omnipotent, who so carest for every one of us as if You cared for him only, and so for all as if they were but one!
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2026-10-10.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twenty-Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: To-day was born the Child, and His name was called Wonder! For a wonder it is that God as a Babe should show Himself.
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

quote.text: Love is the light—and in the end, the only light—that can always illuminate a world grown dim and give us the courage needed to keep living and working.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2026-10-12.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twenty-Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It is the conscience of the offerer that sanctifies the sacrifice when it is pure, and thus moves God to accept [the offering] as from a friend.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2026-10-13.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twenty-Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Charity is the form of the virtues.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

quote.text: Therefore as long as a thing has being, God must be present to it, according to its mode of being.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-01-18.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Let us therefore, brethren beloved, pray as God our Teacher has taught us. It is a loving and friendly prayer to beseech God with His own word, to come up to His ears in the prayer of Christ. Let the Father acknowledge the words of His Son when we make our prayer, and let Him also who dwells within in our breast Himself dwell in our voice.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2027-01-19.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Oh, night that guided me, Oh, night more lovely than the dawn, Oh, night that joined Beloved with lover, Lover transformed in the Beloved!
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2027-01-20.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He can no longer have God for his Father, who has not the Church for his mother.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2027-01-21.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Whosoever praises God for His essential goodness, and not merely because of the benefits He has bestowed, does really love God for God’s sake, and not selfishly.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2027-01-22.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Thus we say that He is worshipped both by ourselves and by the holy angels, not unwisely bringing Him down to a mere humanity, but following the divine Scripture, and binding the Word who was begotten of God to our nature in a union, and weaving the product of both into a certain unity, so that He might not be considered as a mere man who bore God, but rather as God who became man.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria

2027-01-23.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: To save our soul, one thing alone is necessary: to hear the word of God and to live by it. Therein lies the best part, which will not be taken away from a faithful soul even though it should lose everything else.
quote.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP

quote.text: We have been taught, and are convinced, and do believe, that He accepts those only who imitate the excellences which reside in Him, temperance, and justice, and philanthropy, and as many virtues as are peculiar to a God who is called by no proper name. And we have been taught that He in the beginning did of His goodness, for man's sake, create all things out of unformed matter.
quote.author: St. Justin Martyr

2027-01-25.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time
2027-01-25.celebration: The Conversion of St. Paul, Apostle

quote.text: Change of place does not effect any drawing nearer unto God, but wherever you may be, God will come to you, if the chambers of your soul be found of such a sort that He can dwell in you and walk in you.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa

2027-01-26.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Our God was not satisfied to be among us only by means of the inestimable mystery of the incarnation, by means of which God became man to allow mankind to share in his divine nature, but wanted to go further in an incomprehensible and infallible manner by means of the most divine sacrament of the altar in which truthfully speaking God is with us.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

2027-01-27.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: My sins run out behind me, and I do not see them, and today I am coming to judge the errors of another.
quote.author: Abba Moses

2027-01-28.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time
2027-01-28.celebration: St. Thomas Aquinas, Priest and Doctor

quote.text: The presence of Christ's true body and blood in this sacrament cannot be detected by sense, nor understanding, but by faith alone, which rests upon Divine authority.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-01-29.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Behave as if you were a stranger, and wherever you are, do not expect your words to have an influence and you will be at peace.
quote.author: Abba Poemen

2027-01-30.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: All waters, therefore, in virtue of the pristine privilege of their origin, do, after invocation of God, attain the sacramental power of sanctification; for the Spirit immediately supervenes from the heavens, and rests over the waters, sanctifying them from Himself.
quote.author: Tertullian

quote.text: For the new grace of God has renewed all things from carnal unto spiritual, by superinducing the Gospel, the obliterator of the whole ancient bygone system; in which our Lord Jesus Christ has been approved as the Spirit of God, and the Word of God, and the Reason of God: the Spirit, by which He was mighty; the Word, by which He taught; the Reason, by which He came.
quote.author: Tertullian

2027-02-01.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It is the Father to Whom all existence owes its origin. In Christ and through Christ He is the source of all. In contrast to all else He is self-existent. He does not draw His being from without, but possesses it from Himself and in Himself. He is infinite, for nothing contains Him and He contains all things; He is eternally unconditioned by space, for He is illimitable; eternally anterior to time, for time is His creation.
quote.author: St. Hilary of Poitiers

2027-02-02.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The beginning of discipline is humility: first, that he hold no knowledge, no scripture as vile; second, that he not be ashamed to learn from anyone; third, that when he shall have attained knowledge, he not contemn the others.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

2027-02-03.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: When through love the mind is ravished by Divine knowledge and in going outside of creatures has a perception of Divine Transcendence, then it comes in consternation to a realization of its own lowliness.
quote.author: St. Maximus the Confessor

2027-02-04.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: This is part of the infinite goodness of God, that He should allow evil to exist, and out of it produce good.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-02-05.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Too late loved I Thee, O Thou Beauty of ancient days, yet ever new! too late I loved Thee!
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-02-06.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The time which you lend to God is not lost: he will return it to you with large interest.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

quote.text: Also in this He shewed me a little thing, the quantity of an hazel-nut, in the palm of my hand; and it was as round as a ball. I looked thereupon with eye of my understanding, and thought: What may this be? And it was answered generally thus: It is all that is made. I marvelled how it might last, for methought it might suddenly have fallen to naught for little[ness]. And I was answered in my understanding: It lasteth, and ever shall [last] for that God loveth it. And so All-thing hath the Being by the love of God.
quote.author: Julian of Norwich

2027-02-08.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: There we shall rest and see, see and love, love and praise. This is what shall be in the end without end.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-02-09.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For piety is an insatiable thing; and it argues a childish mind to imagine from just the beginnings that you have obtained the whole.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

quote.text: Do you fast? Give me proof of it by your works. If you see a poor man, take pity on him.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

quote.text: O sweet exchange! O unsearchable operation! O benefits surpassing all expectation! That the wickedness of many should be hid in a single righteous One, and that the righteousness of One should justify many transgressors!
quote.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus

quote.text: We needed an Incarnate God, a God put to death, that we might live.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

quote.text: For that death is destroyed, and that the Cross has become the victory over it, and that it has no more power but is verily dead, this is no small proof, or rather an evident warrant, that it is despised by all Christ's disciples, and that they all take the aggressive against it and no longer fear it; but by the sign of the Cross and by faith in Christ tread it down as dead.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

quote.text: Strive always to prefer, not that which is easiest, but that which is most difficult; Not that which is most delectable, but that which is most unpleasing; Not that which gives most pleasure, but rather that which gives least; Not that which is restful, but that which is wearisome.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2027-02-15.seasonLabel: Monday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: The whole of our treatment and exertion is concerned with the hidden man of the heart, and our warfare is directed against that adversary and foe within us, who uses ourselves as his weapons against ourselves, and, most fearful of all, hands us over to the death of sin.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-02-16.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: Dost thou not know, dear daughter, that all the sufferings, which the soul endures, or can endure, in this life, are insufficient to punish one smallest fault, because the offence, being done to Me, who am the Infinite Good, calls for an infinite satisfaction?
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2027-02-17.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: Go and sit in your cell and your cell will teach you everything.
quote.author: Abba Moses

2027-02-18.seasonLabel: Thursday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: From this love-o'erflowing Passion, I take what fully makes up for the insufficiency of my own small merits; herein lies my complete justification; to meditate on this Passion, I call eternal wisdom, the perfection of all knowledge, the riches of all salvation, an entire satisfaction of all desert; it casts me down in prosperity, it raises me up in adversity, it keeps me in an even balance between this world's weal and woe, and guards me against all evil in complete security.
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

2027-02-19.seasonLabel: Friday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: So it behoves us, if we would have Christ for a frequent guest, to fill our hearts with faithful meditations on the mercy He showed in dying for us, and on His mighty power in rising again from the dead.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2027-02-20.seasonLabel: Saturday of the First Week of Lent

quote.text: A soul does not benefit as it should from the sacrament of confession if it is not humble. Pride keeps it in darkness. The soul neither knows how, nor is it willing, to probe with precision the depths of its own misery. It puts on a mask and avoids everything that might bring it recovery.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

quote.text: Fasting is the soul of prayer, mercy is the lifeblood of fasting. Let no one try to separate them; they cannot be separated. If you have only one of them or not all together, you have nothing. So if you pray, fast; if you fast, show mercy; if you want your petition to be heard, hear the petition of others.
quote.author: St. Peter Chrysologus

2027-02-22.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week of Lent
2027-02-22.celebration: The Chair of St. Peter, Apostle

quote.text: Oh, what a sure path is the way of Thy Passion, along the way of truth, up to the very pinnacle of all perfection.
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

2027-02-23.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: If you don't deny yourself you will never be a soul of prayer.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2027-02-24.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: For if we too live as though dying daily, we shall not sin.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

2027-02-25.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: Let us then not be ashamed of the Cross of our Saviour, but rather glory in it.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2027-02-26.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: A man is born natural and physical. In baptism he becomes supernatural and theological.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

2027-02-27.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week of Lent

quote.text: It is only by conforming our own will to the divine one that human beings attain their true height, that they become 'divine'; only by coming out of ourselves, only in the 'yes' to God, is Adam's desire — and the desire of us all — to be completely free.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

quote.text: For the wilderness is the mother of quiet; it is a calm and a harbor, delivering us from all turmoils.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2027-03-01.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: He who had wished to demonstrate to us, even in His own flesh, the flesh's infirmity, by the reality of suffering, said, 'Father, remove this Your cup'; and remembering Himself, added, 'save that not my will, but Yours be done.' He was the Will and the Power of the Father: and yet, for the demonstration of the patience which was due, He gave Himself up to the Father's Will.
quote.author: Tertullian

2027-03-02.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: When the body therefore fasts from food, let the mind fast from vices, and pass judgment upon all earthly cares and desires according to the law of its King.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2027-03-03.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: God was born to take us into Himself, suffered to justify us, and died to avenge us; for our manhood abides for ever in Him, the weakness of our infirmity is united with His strength, and the spiritual powers of iniquity and wickedness are subdued in the triumph of our flesh, since God died through the flesh.
quote.author: St. Hilary of Poitiers

2027-03-04.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: Take my advice, my friend, and be slow to do evil, but swift to your salvation; for readiness to evil and tardiness to good are equally bad.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-03-05.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: O Lord and Master of my life, give me not the spirit of sloth, of despondency, of ambition, or of idle talking; but bestow on me, Thy servant, the spirit of chastity, of humility, of patience, and of love.
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

2027-03-06.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week of Lent

quote.text: More easily through fasting does prayer penetrate heaven.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

quote.text: Offer your soul to God, make him an oblation of your fasting, so that your soul may be a pure offering, a holy sacrifice, a living victim, remaining your own and at the same time made over to God. Whoever fails to give this to God will not be excused, for if you are to give him yourself you are never without the means of giving.
quote.author: St. Peter Chrysologus

2027-03-08.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: As you have entered upon a good and most glorious path, run with reverence the race of godliness.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2027-03-09.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: In the distribution of alms and care of the poor, let our Christian fast-times be fat and abound; and let each bestow on the weak and destitute those dainties which he denies himself.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2027-03-10.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: Turn thee with thy whole heart unto the Lord, and forsake this wretched world, and thy soul shall find rest. Learn to despise outward things and to give thyself to things inward, and thou shalt see the kingdom of God come within thee.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2027-03-11.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: O sweet Jesus, wound my heart, that tears of penitence and love may be my food night and day, and bring me entirely to thee; that my heart may ever be habitable for thee, and my conversation pleasing and acceptable to thee; and the end of my life so praiseworthy, that after the close of this life, I may deserve to praise thee with all thy Saints forever.
quote.author: St. Bridget of Sweden

2027-03-12.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: The life of a monk ought at all times to have about it a Lenten character.
quote.author: St. Benedict of Nursia

2027-03-13.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week of Lent

quote.text: Whoever is desirous of great reward and everlasting salvation, of high knowledge and deep wisdom, of standing erect in joy and sorrow, of possessing full security against all evil, whoever wishes to drink a draught of Thy bitter Passion, and Thy singular sweetness, must carry Thee at all times, O crucified Jesus, before the eyes of his heart.
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

quote.text: Behold, assiduous meditation on My Passion makes out of a simple man a master of high knowledge; truly it is a living book in which everything is to be found. How right blessed is that man who has it ever before his eyes and studies it!
quote.author: Bl. Henry Suso

2027-03-15.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: Take therefore first, as an indestructible foundation, the Cross, and build upon it the other articles of the faith.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2027-03-16.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: There are three things, my brethren, by which faith stands firm, devotion remains constant, and virtue endures. They are prayer, fasting and mercy. Prayer knocks at the door, fasting obtains, mercy receives. Prayer, mercy and fasting: these three are one, and they give life to each other.
quote.author: St. Peter Chrysologus

2027-03-17.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: He who is tormented by the passions of the soul, with the adversary lying in wait, should not therefore believe himself to be alienated from Christ because he suffers such things; but rather let him think that by this he is commendable to God, if, while he suffers these things, he praises God rather, not accuses.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

2027-03-18.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: Wouldst thou learn thy Lord's meaning in this thing? Learn it well: Love was His meaning. Who shewed it thee? Love. What shewed He thee? Love. Wherefore shewed it He? For Love. Hold thee therein and thou shalt learn and know more in the same. But thou shalt never know nor learn therein other thing without end. Thus was I learned that Love was our Lord's meaning.
quote.author: Julian of Norwich

2027-03-19.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifth Week of Lent
2027-03-19.celebration: St. Joseph, Spouse of the Blessed Virgin Mary

quote.text: There are two things, if I may speak summarily, which have been divinely handed down to us concerning the Virgin: that She was the Spouse of God and Mother of Christ.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

2027-03-20.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifth Week of Lent

quote.text: Poverty, to those who bear it wisely, is a great possession, a treasure that cannot be taken away; the stoutest of staves; a way of gain that cannot be thwarted; a lodging that is safe from snares.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2027-03-21.celebration: Palm Sunday of the Passion of the Lord

quote.text: In that way the first part is humility; the second, humility; the third, humility: and this I would continue to repeat as often as you might ask direction, not that there are no other instructions which may be given, but because, unless humility precede, accompany, and follow every good action which we perform, being at once the object which we keep before our eyes, the support to which we cling, and the monitor by which we are restrained, pride wrests wholly from our hand any good work on which we are congratulating ourselves.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

quote.text: It belongs to the wise man to meditate especially on the truth belonging to the first principle and to teach it to others, so it belongs to him to refute the opposing falsehood.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

quote.text: Blessed art thou, my Lord, my God, and most beloved lover of my soul, who art one God in three persons.
quote.author: St. Bridget of Sweden

quote.text: Remember how important it is for you to have understood this truth—that the Lord is within us and that we should be there with Him.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

2027-03-25.celebration: Holy Thursday — Mass of the Lord's Supper

quote.text: Thou that instructeth Christians in Thy heavenly wisdom! Guide us to that topmost height of mystic lore which exceedeth light and more than exceedeth knowledge, where the simple, absolute, and unchangeable mysteries of heavenly Truth lie hidden in the dazzling obscurity of the secret Silence, outshining all brilliance with the intensity of their darkness.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2027-03-26.celebration: Good Friday of the Passion of the Lord

quote.text: Mercy, also, is a good thing, for it makes men perfect, in that it imitates the perfect Father. Nothing graces the Christian soul so much as mercy; mercy as shown chiefly towards the poor, that you may treat them as sharers in common with you in the produce of nature, which brings forth the fruits of the earth for use to all.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

quote.text: All the pleasures of the world, and all the kingdoms of this earth, shall profit me nothing. It is better for me to die on behalf of Jesus Christ, than to reign over all the ends of the earth.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2027-03-28.celebration: Easter Sunday of the Resurrection of the Lord

quote.text: Do not be afraid of Christ! He takes nothing away, and he gives you everything.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2027-03-29.seasonLabel: Monday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: We say that the Resurrection is the reconstitution of our nature in its original form.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa

2027-03-30.seasonLabel: Tuesday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: Love the one good in which are all goods, and it sufficeth. Desire the simple good which is every good, and it is enough.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2027-03-31.seasonLabel: Wednesday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: For by the Word of God being made man has come about the destruction of death and the resurrection of life.
quote.author: St. Athanasius

2027-04-01.seasonLabel: Thursday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: This is a Feast of feasts and a Solemnity of solemnities as far exalted above all others (not only those which are merely human and creep on the ground, but even those which are of Christ Himself, and are celebrated in His honour) as the Sun is above the stars.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-04-02.seasonLabel: Friday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: This is the Halleluia which we sing, which, as you know, meaneth, Praise ye the Lord. Therefore that period is before the Lord's Resurrection, this, after His Resurrection: by which time is signified the future hope which as yet we have not: for what we represent after the Lord's Resurrection, we shall have after our own.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-04-03.seasonLabel: Saturday within the Octave of Easter

quote.text: Yesterday I was crucified with Him; today I am glorified with Him. Yesterday I died with Him; today I am quickened with Him.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-04-04.celebration: Divine Mercy Sunday (Second Sunday of Easter)

quote.text: All grace flows from mercy, and the last hour abounds with mercy for us. Let no one doubt concerning the goodness of God; even if a person's sins were as dark as night, God's mercy is stronger than our misery. One thing alone is necessary: that the sinner set ajar the door of his heart, be it ever so little, to let in a ray of God's merciful grace, and then God will do the rest.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

2027-04-05.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: I am love and Mercy Itself. There is no misery that could be a match for My mercy, neither will misery exhaust it, because as it is being granted—it increases. The soul that trusts in My mercy is most fortunate, because I Myself take care of it.
quote.author: St. Faustina Kowalska

2027-04-06.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: And so, Lord, do thou, who dost give understanding to faith, give me, so far as thou knowest it to be profitable, to understand that thou art as we believe; and that thou art that which we believe.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2027-04-07.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: Hell took a body, and met God face to face. It took earth, and encountered Heaven.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2027-04-08.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: The soul, who is lifted by a very great and yearning desire for the honour of God and the salvation of souls, begins by exercising herself, for a certain space of time, in the ordinary virtues, remaining in the cell of self-knowledge, in order to know better the goodness of God towards her.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2027-04-09.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: What art thou, except that which, as the highest of all beings, alone exists through itself, and creates all other things from nothing?
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2027-04-10.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week of Easter

quote.text: We are an Easter People and Alleluia is our song!
quote.author: St. John Paul II

quote.text: For thus we have been enriched by His poverty, the human nature having been brought up in Him to a God-befitting dignity, and established on thrones beyond all things.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria

2027-04-12.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: No virtue, my daughter, can have life in itself except through charity and humility, which is the foster-mother and nurse of charity.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2027-04-13.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: Hence, there is no doubt that there exists a being, than which nothing greater can be conceived, and it exists both in the understanding and in reality.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2027-04-14.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: Until I enter into thy joy, O Lord, who art the Three and the One God, blessed for ever and ever. Amen.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2027-04-15.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: It was the first day of a new creation, with a new heaven and a new earth; and in a semblance of the gardener God walked again in the garden, in the cool not of the evening but the dawn.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2027-04-16.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: Moreover, in the Catholic Church itself, all possible care must be taken, that we hold that faith which has been believed everywhere, always, by all. For that is truly and in the strictest sense Catholic, which, as the name itself and the reason of the thing declare, comprehends all universally. This rule we shall observe if we follow universality, antiquity, consent.
quote.author: St. Vincent of Lérins

2027-04-17.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week of Easter

quote.text: The Spirit of Truth Himself makes the house of His glory shine with the brightness of His light, and will have nothing dark nor lukewarm in His temple.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

quote.text: Thou hast found him to be a being which is the highest of all beings, a being than which nothing better can be conceived; that this being is life itself, light, wisdom, goodness, eternal blessedness and blessed eternity; and that it is every where and always.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

2027-04-19.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: The common belief of all mankind agrees that God, the supreme of all things, is good. For since nothing can be imagined better than God, how can we doubt Him to be good than whom there is nothing better?
quote.author: St. Boethius

2027-04-20.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: But You, my Love, in looking for whom I fail that I may be strong, art neither those bodies that we see, although in heaven, nor are You those which we see not there; for You have created them, nor do You reckon them among Your greatest works. But You are the life of souls, the life of lives, having life in Yourself; and You change not, O Life of my soul.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-04-21.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: For progress requires that the subject be enlarged in itself, alteration, that it be transformed into something else. The intelligence, then, the knowledge, the wisdom, as well of individuals as of all, as well of one man as of the whole Church, ought, in the course of ages and centuries, to increase and make much and vigorous progress; but yet only in its own kind; that is to say, in the same doctrine, in the same sense, and in the same meaning.
quote.author: St. Vincent of Lérins

2027-04-22.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: As water extinguishes fire, so almsgiving quenches sin. Here also it is shown and proved, that as in the layer of saving water the fire of Gehenna is extinguished, so by almsgiving and works of righteousness the flame of sins is subdued.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2027-04-23.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: For as strolling, walking and running are bodily exercises, so every way of preparing and disposing the soul to rid itself of all the disordered tendencies, and, after it is rid, to seek and find the Divine Will as to the management of one's life for the salvation of the soul, is called a Spiritual Exercise.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

2027-04-24.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourth Week of Easter

quote.text: He makes himself the hungry one – the naked one – the homeless one – the sick one – the one in prison – the lonely one – the unwanted one – and he says: You did it to me.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

quote.text: As there are many rays of the sun, but one light; and many branches of a tree, but one strength based in its tenacious root; and since from one spring flow many streams, although the multiplicity seems diffused in the liberality of an overflowing abundance, yet the unity is still preserved in the source.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2027-04-26.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: What is wrong is that we do not ask what is right.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2027-04-27.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: Of all, then, that has been said since we entered upon the discussion about things, this is the sum: that we should clearly understand that the fulfillment and the end of the Law, and of all Holy Scripture, is the love of an object which is to be enjoyed, and the love of an object which can enjoy that other in fellowship with ourselves.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-04-28.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: The highest perfection, therefore, of man in this life lies in this: that he is so united to God that his soul with all its powers and faculties becomes recollected in Him and is one spirit with Him.
quote.author: St. Albert the Great

2027-04-29.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifth Week of Easter
2027-04-29.celebration: St. Catherine of Siena, Virgin and Doctor

quote.text: If you are what you ought to be, you will set fire to all Italy.
quote.author: St. Catherine of Siena

2027-04-30.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: To-day was born the Child, and His name was called Wonder! For a wonder it is that God as a Babe should show Himself.
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

2027-05-01.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifth Week of Easter

quote.text: Of all the practices of the spiritual life purity of heart stands highest, and rightly, for it is the end and reward of all our labours, and is found only with those who live truly according to the spirit and as good religious.
quote.author: St. Albert the Great

quote.text: Illumination is the greatest and most magnificent of the Gifts of God.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-05-03.seasonLabel: Monday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: The contemplative life is to cling with our whole mind to the love of God and our neighbor, and to desire nothing beside our Creator.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-05-04.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: They abstain from the Eucharist and from prayer, because they confess not the Eucharist to be the flesh of our Saviour Jesus Christ, which suffered for our sins, and which the Father, of His goodness, raised up again.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2027-05-05.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: The supreme wisdom is this, to work at the exclusion of all self-seeking, and to be ready to do the will of God.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2027-05-06.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: May those, then, who are good, chaste, sober, humble, and gentle, persevere with God's help in their good works; may those who do evil correct themselves at once before their souls depart from this life. If they die without repentance they do not enter into life, but are cast into death.
quote.author: St. Caesarius of Arles

2027-05-07.seasonLabel: Friday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: I am wont to visit My elect in two ways—by temptation and by consolation.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2027-05-08.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Sixth Week of Easter

quote.text: And by thought it is that He creates, and thought is the basis of the work, the Word filling it and the Spirit perfecting it.
quote.author: St. John of Damascus

quote.text: Love begins at home, and it is not how much we do, but how much love we put in the action that we do.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

2027-05-10.seasonLabel: Monday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: It behoved that there should be sin; but all shall be well, and all shall be well, and all manner of thing shall be well.
quote.author: Julian of Norwich

2027-05-11.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: This Good is celebrated by the sacred theologians, both as beautiful and as Beauty, and as Love, and as Beloved; and all the other Divine Names which beseem the beautifying and highly-favoured comeliness.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2027-05-12.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: Though I am poor, Lord, I give thee all I possess; but when I say I give thee my will, I have nothing left to give thee.
quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

2027-05-13.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: Only in this friendship are the doors of life opened wide. Only in this friendship is the great potential of human existence truly revealed. Only in this friendship do we experience beauty and liberation.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2027-05-14.seasonLabel: Friday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: Maker of earth and sky, from age to age / Who rul'st the world by reason; at whose word / Time issues from Eternity's abyss.
quote.author: St. Boethius

2027-05-15.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Seventh Week of Easter

quote.text: The believer has his stand on the Rock; for this reason he cannot be overthrown by the dashing of the billows.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

quote.text: This gift is with us unto the end of the world, the solace of our waiting, the assurance, by the favours which He bestows, of the hope that shall be ours, the light of our minds, the sun of our souls. This Holy Spirit we must seek and must earn, and then hold fast by faith and obedience to the commands of God.
quote.author: St. Hilary of Poitiers

2027-05-17.seasonLabel: Monday of the Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Final and perfect happiness can consist in nothing else than the vision of the Divine Essence.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-05-18.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: As soon as some infirmity overtakes him, a sick man should receive the Body and Blood of Christ, humbly and devoutly ask the presbyters for blessed oil, and anoint his body with it. Thus will be fulfilled in him what we read: 'Is anyone among you sick? Let him bring in the presbyters, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil. And the prayer of faith will save the sick man, and the Lord will raise him up, and if he be in sins, they shall be forgiven him.' See to it, brethren, that a man hastens to the health and the remission of his sins.
quote.author: St. Caesarius of Arles

2027-05-19.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He, therefore, who is not illumined by such great splendor of created things is blind; he who is not awakened by such great clamor is deaf; he who does not praise God because of all these effects is dumb; he who does not note the First Principle from such great signs is foolish.
quote.author: St. Bonaventure

2027-05-20.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The highest solace in life is the study of wisdom, and he who finds it is fortunate, and he who possesses it is blessed.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

2027-05-21.seasonLabel: Friday of the Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The whole world cannot afford thee peace, but the peace of God can.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2027-05-22.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Seventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Mary had the greatest need of Christ as Redeemer, for she would have contracted original sin by reason of her descent unless this had been prevented by the grace of a Mediator. As others needed Christ that through His merit sin already contracted might be remitted to them, she needed even more a Mediator preventing this, that she might not at any time be under debt of contracting it, and that she be preserved from actually contracting it.
quote.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus

quote.text: It is better for a man to be silent and be [a Christian], than to talk and not to be one. It is good to teach, if he who speaks also acts. There is then one Teacher, who spoke and it was done; while even those things which He did in silence are worthy of the Father.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2027-05-24.seasonLabel: Monday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Seek Him in faith and love, without seeking to satisfy thyself in aught, or to understand more than is expedient for thee to know; for faith and love are the two guides of the blind; they will lead thee, by a way thou knowest not, to the secret chamber of God.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2027-05-25.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Neither pray as the hypocrites; but as the Lord commanded in His Gospel, thus pray: Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come. Your will be done, as in heaven, so on earth. Give us today our daily (needful) bread, and forgive us our debt as we also forgive our debtors. And bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one; for Yours is the power and the glory forever. Thrice in the day thus pray.
quote.author: The Didache

2027-05-26.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He who hath greater knowledge, and not the fear of God, is the more severe judge of himself.
quote.author: Thomas à Kempis

2027-05-27.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time
2027-05-27.celebration: The Most Holy Body and Blood of Christ (Corpus Christi)

quote.text: But every Lord's day gather yourselves together, and break bread, and give thanksgiving after having confessed your transgressions, that your sacrifice may be pure. But let no one that is at variance with his fellow come together with you, until they be reconciled, that your sacrifice may not be profaned.
quote.author: The Didache

2027-05-28.seasonLabel: Friday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It is precisely Mary's faith which marks the beginning of the new and eternal Covenant of God with man in Jesus Christ; this heroic faith of hers 'precedes' the apostolic witness of the Church, and ever remains in the Church's heart hidden like a special heritage of God's revelation.
quote.author: St. John Paul II

2027-05-29.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Eighth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I do not seek to understand that I may believe, but I believe in order to understand.
quote.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

quote.text: What the soul is to the human body, the Holy Spirit is to the Body of Christ, which is the Church.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-05-31.seasonLabel: Monday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time
2027-05-31.celebration: The Visitation of the Blessed Virgin Mary

quote.text: All progress proceeds from reading and meditation. For the things we do not know, we learn by reading; but the things we have learned, we preserve by meditations.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

2027-06-01.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Creation proclaims outright the Creator; for the very heavens, as the Prophet says, declare the glory of God with their unutterable words.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa

2027-06-02.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: These things therefore being manifest to us, and since we look into the depths of the divine knowledge, it behooves us to do all things in [their proper] order, which the Lord has commanded us to perform at stated times. He has enjoined offerings [to be presented] and service to be performed [to Him], and that not thoughtlessly or irregularly, but at the appointed times and hours. Where and by whom He desires these things to be done, He Himself has fixed by His own supreme will, in order that all things, being piously done according to His good pleasure, may be acceptable unto Him.
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

2027-06-03.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For peace is a good so great, that even in this earthly and mortal life there is no word we hear with such pleasure, nothing we desire with such zest, or find to be more thoroughly gratifying.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-06-04.seasonLabel: Friday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time
2027-06-04.celebration: The Most Sacred Heart of Jesus

quote.text: The study of inspired Scripture is the chief way of finding our duty, for in it we find both instruction about conduct and the lives of blessed men, delivered in writing, as some breathing images of godly living, for the imitation of their good works.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

2027-06-05.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Ninth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: All mortal creatures in those anxious aims which find employment in so many varied pursuits, though they take many paths, yet strive to reach one goal—the goal of happiness.
quote.author: St. Boethius

quote.text: We have all known the long loneliness and we have learned that the only solution is love and that love comes with community.
quote.author: Servant of God Dorothy Day

2027-06-07.seasonLabel: Monday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Man is called to a fullness of life which far exceeds the dimensions of his earthly existence, because it consists in sharing the very life of God.
quote.author: St. John Paul II

2027-06-08.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Prayer is of the heart, not of the lips. For God does not attend to the words of the supplicant, but looks upon the heart of the one praying.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

2027-06-09.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: A person enters into the first tabernacle when he returns to himself. A person enters into the second tabernacle when he goes beyond himself. When going beyond himself surely a person is elevated to God.
quote.author: Richard of St. Victor

2027-06-10.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Deity, therefore, is ineffable and incomprehensible.
quote.author: St. John of Damascus

2027-06-11.seasonLabel: Friday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God always was and always is, and always will be; or rather, God always Is.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-06-12.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Tenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Let nothing disturb thee, nothing affright thee; all things are passing; God never changeth; patient endurance attaineth to all things; who God possesseth in nothing is wanting; alone God sufficeth.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

quote.text: If, therefore, those who were brought up in the ancient order of things have come to the possession of a new hope, no longer observing the Sabbath, but living in the observance of the Lord's Day, on which also our life has sprung up again by Him and by His death — whom some deny, by which mystery we have obtained faith, and therefore endure, that we may be found the disciples of Jesus Christ, our only Master.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2027-06-14.seasonLabel: Monday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: By the simplex and supernatural nature of all beautiful things, all beauty, and everything beautiful, pre-existed uniquely as to Cause.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2027-06-15.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I would fain send up a cry so loud that it would put fear in all men on the earth. I would say to them: 'Wretches, why do you let yourselves be thus blinded by the world, you whose need is so great and grievous, as you will know at the moment of death, and who make no provision for it whatsoever?'
quote.author: St. Catherine of Genoa

2027-06-16.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I assert, first, that the natural law is not only indicative of bad and good, but also contains its own proper prohibition of what is bad and prescription of what is good.
quote.author: Francisco Suarez, SJ

2027-06-17.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The divine words grow with the one who reads them.
quote.author: St. Gregory the Great

2027-06-18.seasonLabel: Friday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: How beautiful is the spectacle to God when a Christian does battle with pain; when he is drawn up against threats, and punishments, and tortures; when, mocking the noise of death, he treads under foot the horror of the executioner; when he raises up his liberty against kings and princes, and yields to God alone, whose he is; when, triumphant and victorious, he tramples upon the very man who has pronounced sentence against him!
quote.author: Minucius Felix

2027-06-19.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Eleventh Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: In such wise man, animal and carnal by nature, and loving only himself, begins to love God by reason of that very self-love; since he learns that in God he can accomplish all things that are good, and that without God he can do nothing.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

quote.text: We are not real social workers. We may be doing social work in the eyes of the people, but we are really contemplatives in the heart of the world.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

2027-06-21.seasonLabel: Monday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The one who loves God cannot help but love also every man as himself even though he is displeased by the passions of those who are not yet purified.
quote.author: St. Maximus the Confessor

2027-06-22.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Don't wish to be like the gilded weather-cock on top of a great building: however much it shines, and however high it stands, it adds nothing to the solidity of the building. Rather be like an old stone block hidden in the foundations, underground, where no one can see you: because of you the house will not fall.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2027-06-23.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For as the bread, which is produced from the earth, when it receives the invocation of God, is no longer common bread, but the Eucharist, consisting of two realities, earthly and heavenly; so also our bodies, when they receive the Eucharist, are no longer corruptible, having the hope of the resurrection to eternity.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2027-06-24.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time
2027-06-24.celebration: The Nativity of St. John the Baptist

quote.text: Paul, when enumerating the innumerable causes which generally separate men from the love of Christ and from the love of God in Christ Jesus, to all of which the love that was in himself rose superior, did not set down argument among the grounds of separation. For he says: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword? Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through Him that loved us.
quote.author: Origen of Alexandria

2027-06-25.seasonLabel: Friday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The souls who are in Purgatory cannot, as I understand, choose but be there, and this is by God's ordinance who therein has done justly. They cannot turn their thoughts back to themselves, nor can they say, "Such sins I have committed for which I deserve to be here", nor, "I would that I had not committed them for then I would go now to Paradise."
quote.author: St. Catherine of Genoa

2027-06-26.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twelfth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Love finds nothing hard; no task is difficult to the eager. Think of all that Jacob bore for Rachel, the wife who had been promised to him. And they seemed unto him but a few days for the love he had to her.
quote.author: St. Jerome

quote.text: Every fortune is good fortune; for it either rewards, disciplines, amends, or punishes, and so is either useful or just.
quote.author: St. Boethius

2027-06-28.seasonLabel: Monday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time
2027-06-28.celebration: St. Irenaeus, Bishop, Martyr and Doctor

quote.text: The oblation of the Church, therefore, which the Lord gave instructions to be offered throughout all the world, is accounted with God a pure sacrifice, and is acceptable to Him; not that He stands in need of a sacrifice from us, but that he who offers is himself glorified in what he does offer, if his gift be accepted. For by the gift both honour and affection are shown forth towards the King.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2027-06-29.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Blessed is the man who has learnt to love all men equally.
quote.author: St. Maximus the Confessor

2027-06-30.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: It is a matter of necessity that every Church should agree with this Church, on account of its preeminent authority [potiorem principalitatem].
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2027-07-01.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The Father sent the Son to preserve us and give us life, in order that He might restore us; and the Son was willing to be sent and to become the Son of man, that He might make us sons of God; humbled Himself, that He might raise up the people who before were prostrate; was wounded that He might heal our wounds; served, that He might draw out to liberty those who were in bondage; underwent death, that He might set forth immortality to mortals.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

2027-07-02.seasonLabel: Friday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Burst forth into a fountain of abundance and overflow with mystical knowledge, until they who now think you contemptible because of Eve's transgression are stirred up by the flood of your irrigation.
quote.author: St. Hildegard of Bingen

2027-07-03.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Thirteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: You cannot love a thing without wanting to fight for it. You cannot fight without something to fight for. To love a thing without wishing to fight for it is not love at all; it is lust.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

quote.text: Therefore I maintain, my daughters, that we should fix our eyes on Christ our only good, and on His saints; there we shall learn true humility.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

2027-07-05.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The outcry is that the State is filled with Christians — that they are in the fields, in the citadels, in the islands: they make lamentation, as for some calamity, that both sexes, every age and condition, even high rank, are passing over to the profession of the Christian faith; and yet for all, their minds are not awakened to the thought of some good they have failed to notice in it.
quote.author: Tertullian

2027-07-06.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: O chaste and holy love! O sweet and gracious affection! O pure and cleansed purpose, thoroughly washed and purged from any admixture of selfishness, and sweetened by contact with the divine will!
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2027-07-07.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Happy is our sacrament of water, in that, by washing away the sins of our early blindness, we are set free and admitted into eternal life! But we, little fishes, after the example of our ΙΧΘΥΣ Jesus Christ, are born in water, nor have we safety in any other way than by permanently abiding in water.
quote.author: Tertullian

2027-07-08.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Even as this broken bread was scattered over the hills, and was gathered together and became one, so let Your Church be gathered together from the ends of the earth into Your kingdom; for Yours is the glory and the power through Jesus Christ forever.
quote.author: The Didache

2027-07-09.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The people most tempted are those who are ready, with the grace of God, to sacrifice everything for the salvation of their poor souls, who renounce all those things which most people eagerly seek. It is not one devil only who tempts them, but millions seek to entrap them.
quote.author: St. John Vianney

2027-07-10.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fourteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: But how great is the Lord's indulgence! How great His condescension and plenteousness of goodness towards us, seeing that He has wished us to pray in the sight of God in such a way as to call God Father, and to call ourselves sons of God, even as Christ is the Son of God — a name which none of us would dare to venture on in prayer, unless He Himself had allowed us thus to pray!
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

quote.text: It follows that if there is any grace in the water, it is not of the nature of the water, but of the presence of the Spirit.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

2027-07-12.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: This is the way, beloved, in which we find our Saviour, even Jesus Christ, the High Priest of all our offerings, the defender and helper of our infirmity. By Him we look up to the heights of heaven. By Him we behold, as in a glass, His immaculate and most excellent visage. By Him are the eyes of our hearts opened. By Him our foolish and darkened understanding blossoms up anew towards His marvellous light.
quote.author: St. Clement of Rome

2027-07-13.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: His members might learn to fly from the favours of the world, to be afraid of no terrors, to love adversity for the truth's sake, and to shrink in fear from prosperity; because this often defiles the heart through vain glory, while that purges it through sorrow; in this the mind exalts itself, but in that, even though it had once exalted itself, it brings itself low; in this man forgets himself, but in that, even perforce and against his will, he is recalled to memory of what he is.
quote.author: St. Gregory the Great

2027-07-14.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Thus it is that God, by loving us, restores us to His image, and, in order that He may find in us the form of His goodness, He gives us that whereby we ourselves too may do the work that He does, kindling that is the lamps of our minds, and inflaming us with the fire of His love, that we may love not only Himself, but also whatever He loves.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2027-07-15.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: God meant things to be much easier than we have made them. A man has a natural right to food, clothing, and shelter. A certain amount of goods is necessary to lead a good life. A family needs work as well as bread. Property is proper to man. We must keep repeating these things. Eternal life begins now.
quote.author: Servant of God Dorothy Day

2027-07-16.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Christ is the morning star, who promises and reveals to the saints the eternal light of life, when the night of the world is past.
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

2027-07-17.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Fifteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Everyone who is wise according to God is blessed. The blessed life is the cognition of divinity. The cognition of divinity is the virtue of good work. The virtue of good work is the fruit of eternity.
quote.author: St. Isidore of Seville

quote.text: Whenever we cover our brother's sin, God will cover ours; whenever we tell people about our brother's guilt, God will do the same about ours.
quote.author: Abba Poemen

2027-07-19.seasonLabel: Monday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: There are some who pass through life as through a tunnel, without ever understanding the splendour, the security and the warmth of the sun of faith.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2027-07-20.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Faith and reason are like two wings on which the human spirit rises to the contemplation of truth; and God has placed in the human heart a desire to know the truth—in a word, to know himself—so that, by knowing and loving God, men and women may also come to the fullness of truth about themselves.
quote.author: St. John Paul II

2027-07-21.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The three mystical virtues which Christianity has not adopted, but invented, are faith, hope, and charity.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2027-07-22.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The world was not conceived by chance and without reason, but for an useful end and for the great advantage of all beings, since it is really the school where reasonable souls exercise themselves, the training ground where they learn to know God.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

2027-07-23.seasonLabel: Friday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Of all things to be sought, the first is wisdom, in which the form of the perfect good consists.
quote.author: Hugh of St. Victor

2027-07-24.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Sixteenth Week in Ordinary Time

For example, the name wisdom was at first given to human wisdom and was univocal to the wisdoms of all men. Then, when men rose to a knowledge of the divine nature and saw the proportional similitude between us insofar as we are wise and God, they extended the name wisdom to signify in God that to which our wisdom is proportional.

quote.author: St. Gregory the Great

2027-07-30.seasonLabel: Friday of the Seventeenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

quote.text: The sixth, to consider how He suffers all this for my sins, etc.; and what I ought to do and suffer for Him.

quote.author: St. John of Damascus

quote.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

2027-08-05.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Eighteenth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

quote.author: St. John Vianney

quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2027-08-14.celebration: St. Maximilian Kolbe, Priest and Martyr

quote.text: O Helmsman of that ark, be my pilot on the dry land! To it Thou gavest rest in the haven of a mountain; to me give Thou rest also in the haven of my walls!
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

2027-08-15.celebration: The Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary

2027-08-19.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: And there is, further, the most Divine Knowledge of Almighty God, which is known, through not knowing (agnosia) during the union above mind; when the mind, having stood apart from all existing things, then having dismissed also itself, has been made one with the super-luminous rays, thence and there being illuminated by the unsearchable depth of wisdom.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

quote.author: St. Francis de Sales

2027-08-21.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Twentieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I write the way I do because (not though) I am a Catholic. This is a fact and nothing covers it like the bald statement.
quote.author: Flannery O'Connor

quote.author: St. Albert the Great

quote.text: He has reached the citadel of no small intelligence who knows that he cannot penetrate the secrets of God. Then, however, we rightly recognize God, when we deny that we know him perfectly.
