quote.author: St. Robert Bellarmine

2027-10-27.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Thirtieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I think that the Church is the only thing that is going to make the terrible world we are coming to endurable; the only thing that makes the Church endurable is that it is somehow the body of Christ and that on this we are fed.
quote.author: Flannery O'Connor

2027-10-28.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Thirtieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Nothing is worse than envy and malice, nothing more mischievous than vainglory; it is wont to mar ten thousand good things.
quote.author: St. John Chrysostom

2027-10-29.seasonLabel: Friday of the Thirtieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Christ be with me, Christ within me, Christ behind me, Christ before me, Christ beside me, Christ to win me, Christ to comfort and restore me.
quote.author: St. Patrick (attributed)

2027-10-30.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Thirtieth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I know and I have experienced that 'the Kingdom of God is within us.'
quote.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

quote.text: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting. It has been found difficult; and left untried.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2027-11-01.seasonLabel: Monday of the Thirty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The cheerfulness you should have is not the kind we might call physiological good spirits — the happiness of a healthy animal. You must seek something more: the supernatural happiness that comes from the abandonment of everything and the abandonment of yourself into the loving arms of our Father-God.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2027-11-02.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Thirty-First Week in Ordinary Time
2027-11-02.celebration: The Commemoration of All the Faithful Departed (All Souls)

quote.text: COME, then, let us extol the Peace Divine, and Source of conciliation, by hymns of peace! For this it is which unifies all, and engenders, and effects the agreement and fellowship of all.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2027-11-03.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Thirty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Action is worth nothing without prayer: prayer grows in value with sacrifice.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2027-11-04.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Thirty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: If, dearly beloved, we comprehend faithfully and wisely the beginning of our creation, we shall find that man was made in God's image, to the end that he might imitate his Creator, and that our race attains its highest natural dignity, by the form of the Divine goodness being reflected in us, as in a mirror.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2027-11-05.seasonLabel: Friday of the Thirty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: I always took delight in learning, or teaching, or writing.
quote.author: St. Bede the Venerable

2027-11-06.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Thirty-First Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Thou madest us for Thyself, and our heart is restless, until it repose in Thee.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

quote.text: At the close of life you will be examined as to your love.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2027-11-08.seasonLabel: Monday of the Thirty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Virtue is a good quality of the mind, by which we live righteously, of which no one can make bad use, which God works in us, without us.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-11-09.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Thirty-Second Week in Ordinary Time
2027-11-09.celebration: The Dedication of the Lateran Basilica

quote.text: Worship a little in words, but more by your actions, and rather by keeping the Law than by admiring the Lawgiver; show your love for Him by fleeing from wickedness, pursuing after virtue, living in the Spirit, walking in the Spirit, drawing your knowledge from Him.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-11-10.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Thirty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For whosoever does not confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh, is antichrist; and whosoever does not confess the testimony of the cross, is of the devil; and whosoever perverts the oracles of the Lord to his own lusts, and says that there is neither a resurrection nor a judgment, he is the first-born of Satan.
quote.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna

2027-11-11.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Thirty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Our life is hid with Christ in God. Let, then, no one here strive to shine, let none show pride, let none boast. Christ willed not to be known here, He would not that His Name should be preached in the Gospel while He lived on earth. He came to lie hidden from this world. Let us therefore likewise hide our life after the example of Christ, let us shun boastfulness, let us not desire to be made known. It is better to live here in humility, and there in glory.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

2027-11-12.seasonLabel: Friday of the Thirty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Enter ye in at the narrow gate: for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leadeth to destruction, and many there are who go in thereat. How narrow is the gate, and strait is the way that leadeth to life: and few there are that find it!
quote.author: St. Robert Bellarmine

2027-11-13.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Thirty-Second Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: But be meek, since the meek shall inherit the earth. Be long-suffering and pitiful and guileless and gentle and good and always trembling at the words which you have heard. You shall not exalt yourself, nor give over-confidence to your soul. Your soul shall not be joined with lofty ones, but with just and lowly ones shall it have its intercourse. The workings that befall you receive as good, knowing that apart from God nothing comes to pass.
quote.author: The Didache

quote.text: Rest is in Him alone.
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2027-11-15.seasonLabel: Monday of the Thirty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: He could not have commanded anything more beneficial, for this sacrament is the fruit of the tree of life. Anyone who receives this sacrament with the devotion of sincere faith will never taste death. It is a tree of life for those who grasp it, and blessed is he who holds it fast. The man who feeds on Me shall live on account of Me.
quote.author: St. Albert the Great

2027-11-16.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Thirty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Raise your soul above the earth; draw from its natural conformation the rule of your conduct; fix your conversation in heaven.
quote.author: St. Basil the Great

2027-11-17.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Thirty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The priests indeed are good, but the High Priest is better; to whom the holy of holies has been committed, and who alone has been trusted with the secrets of God. He is the door of the Father, by which enter in Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the prophets, and the apostles, and the Church.
quote.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch

2027-11-18.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Thirty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For we ought to think of God even more often than we draw our breath; and if the expression is permissible, we ought to do nothing else.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-11-19.seasonLabel: Friday of the Thirty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: The word of God makes us change our concept of realism: the realist is the one who recognizes in the word of God the foundation of all things.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2027-11-20.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Thirty-Third Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: As often as this life's idle show tries to charm you; as often as you see in the world some vain pomp, transport yourself in mind to Paradise, essay to be now what you will be hereafter.
quote.author: St. Jerome

2027-11-21.celebration: Our Lord Jesus Christ, King of the Universe

quote.text: And on the day called Sunday, all who live in cities or in the country gather together to one place, and the memoirs of the apostles or the writings of the prophets are read.
quote.author: St. Justin Martyr

2027-11-22.seasonLabel: Monday of the Thirty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: By no means then is it permitted to speak, or even to think, anything, concerning the superessential and hidden Deity, beyond those things divinely revealed to us in the sacred Oracles.
quote.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)

2027-11-23.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Thirty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: To preach any doctrine therefore to Catholic Christians other than what they have received never was lawful, never is lawful, never will be lawful: and to anathematize those who preach anything other than what has once been received, always was a duty, always is a duty, always will be a duty.
quote.author: St. Vincent of Lérins

2027-11-24.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Thirty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: Prayer is nothing else than union with God.
quote.author: St. John Vianney

2027-11-25.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Thirty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: We are Christians and Catholics not because we worship a key, but because we have passed a door; and felt the wind that is the trumpet of liberty blow over the land of the living.
quote.author: G. K. Chesterton

2027-11-26.seasonLabel: Friday of the Thirty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: For a most perfect mediator exercises the most perfect mediation possible in regard to some person for whom he mediates. Thus Christ exercised a most perfect act of mediation in regard to some person for whom He was Mediator. In regard to no person did He have a more exalted relationship than to Mary. Such, however, would not have been true had He not preserved Her from original sin.
quote.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus

2027-11-27.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Thirty-Fourth Week in Ordinary Time

quote.text: But let our speech and petition when we pray be under discipline, observing quietness and modesty. Let us consider that we are standing in God's sight. We must please the divine eyes both with the habit of body and with the measure of voice. For God is the hearer, not of the voice, but of the heart.
quote.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage

quote.text: If, then, as is certain, the kingdom of Jesus Christ is to come into the world, it will be but a necessary consequence of the knowledge of the kingdom of the most holy Virgin Mary, who brought Him into the world the first time, and will make His second advent full of splendour.
quote.author: St. Louis de Montfort

2027-11-29.seasonLabel: Monday of the First Week of Advent

quote.text: Our Lady said yes for us all. It was as if the human race were a little dark house, without light or air, locked and latched. The wind of the Spirit had beaten on the door, rattled the windows, tapped on the dark glass with the tiny hands of flowers… But one day, a girl opened the door, and the little house was swept pure and sweet by the wind.
quote.author: Caryll Houselander

2027-11-30.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the First Week of Advent

quote.text: For where can it be well with us without Him, and where ill if He be present?
quote.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

2027-12-01.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the First Week of Advent

quote.text: In order to arrive at having pleasure in everything, Desire to have pleasure in nothing. In order to arrive at possessing everything, Desire to possess nothing. In order to arrive at being everything, Desire to be nothing. In order to arrive at knowing everything, Desire to know nothing.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2027-12-02.seasonLabel: Thursday of the First Week of Advent

quote.text: But our very Life descended hither, and bore our death, and slew it, out of the abundance of His own life.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-12-03.seasonLabel: Friday of the First Week of Advent

quote.text: He is wise, then, who knows how to keep silent. Lastly, the Wisdom of God said: The Lord has given to me the tongue of learning, that I should know when it is good to speak.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

2027-12-04.seasonLabel: Saturday of the First Week of Advent

quote.text: When thy mind dwells upon anything, Thou art ceasing to cast thyself upon the All. For, in order to pass from the all to the All, Thou hast to deny thyself wholly in all. And, when thou comest to possess it wholly, Thou must possess it without desiring anything. For, if thou wilt have anything in having all, Thou hast not thy treasure purely in God.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

quote.text: The one who has hope lives differently; the one who hopes has been granted the gift of a new life.
quote.author: Pope Benedict XVI

2027-12-06.seasonLabel: Monday of the Second Week of Advent

quote.text: Nay, Lord, Your kingdom come with all speed — the prayer of Christians the confusion of the heathen, the exultation of angels, for the sake of which we suffer, nay, rather, for the sake of which we pray!
quote.author: Tertullian

2027-12-07.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Second Week of Advent

quote.text: Before the blessing of the heavenly words another nature is spoken of, after the consecration the Body is signified.
quote.author: St. Ambrose of Milan

2027-12-08.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Second Week of Advent
2027-12-08.celebration: The Immaculate Conception of the Blessed Virgin Mary

quote.text: We preach not one advent only of Christ, but a second also, far more glorious than the former.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem

2027-12-09.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Second Week of Advent

quote.text: Remember, Lord, Your Church, to deliver it from all evil and to make it perfect in Your love, and gather it from the four winds, sanctified for Your kingdom which You have prepared for it; for Yours is the power and the glory forever. Let grace come, and let this world pass away.
quote.author: The Didache

2027-12-10.seasonLabel: Friday of the Second Week of Advent

quote.text: God Himself, who is almighty, the Creator of all things, and invisible, has sent from heaven, and placed among men, [Him who is] the truth, and the holy and incomprehensible Word. As a king sends his son, who is also a king, so sent He Him; as God He sent Him; as to men He sent Him; as a Saviour He sent Him, and as seeking to persuade, not to compel us; for violence has no place in the character of God.
quote.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus

2027-12-11.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Second Week of Advent

quote.text: Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ.
quote.author: St. Jerome

quote.text: It is a greater good to be preserved from evil than to fall into it and afterwards be freed from it. If Christ merited grace and glory for so many souls, who, for these gifts, are indebted to Christ as Mediator, why should no soul be His debtor for the gift of its innocence?
quote.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus

2027-12-13.seasonLabel: Monday of the Third Week of Advent

quote.text: Be not afraid, it is but a pang now and then, and a struggle; a covenant with your eyes, and a fasting in the wilderness, some calm habitual watchfulness, and the hearty effort to obey, and all will be well. Be not afraid. He is most gracious, and will bring you on by little and little.
quote.author: St. John Henry Newman

2027-12-14.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Third Week of Advent
2027-12-14.celebration: St. John of the Cross, Priest and Doctor

quote.text: On this step the soul now walks so anxiously that it seeks the Beloved in all things. When it eats, when it sleeps, when it watches, when it does aught soever, all its care is about the Beloved.
quote.author: St. John of the Cross

2027-12-15.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Third Week of Advent

quote.text: Among the most holy Sacraments of Christ's church the most excellent is the most divine Sacrament of the Eucharist, the Sacrament of Grace, the Sacrament of charity, the Sacrament of love.
quote.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi

2027-12-16.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Third Week of Advent

quote.text: In that contemplation, therefore, God will be all in all; because nothing else but Himself will be required, but it will be sufficient to be enlightened by and to enjoy Him alone.
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

2027-12-17.seasonLabel: Friday of the Third Week of Advent

quote.text: Polycarp also was not only instructed by apostles, and conversed with many who had seen Christ, but was also, by apostles in Asia, appointed bishop of the Church in Smyrna, whom I also saw in my early youth, for he tarried [on earth] a very long time, and, when a very old man, gloriously and most nobly suffering martyrdom, departed this life, having always taught the things which he had learned from the apostles, and which the Church has handed down, and which alone are true.
quote.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons

2027-12-18.seasonLabel: Saturday of the Third Week of Advent

quote.text: O Thou Good Omnipotent, who so carest for every one of us as if You cared for him only, and so for all as if they were but one!
quote.author: St. Augustine of Hippo

quote.text: Since men become happy by the acquisition of happiness, while happiness is very Godship, it is manifest that they become happy by the acquisition of Godship.
quote.author: St. Boethius

2027-12-20.seasonLabel: Monday of the Fourth Week of Advent

quote.text: For that which He has not assumed He has not healed; but that which is united to His Godhead is also saved.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-12-21.seasonLabel: Tuesday of the Fourth Week of Advent

quote.text: Grace does not destroy nature, but perfects it.
quote.author: St. Thomas Aquinas

2027-12-22.seasonLabel: Wednesday of the Fourth Week of Advent

quote.text: Let him never cease from prayer who has once begun it, be his life ever so wicked; for prayer is the way to amend it, and without prayer such amendment will be much more difficult.
quote.author: St. Teresa of Ávila

2027-12-23.seasonLabel: Thursday of the Fourth Week of Advent

quote.text: How I wish your bearing and conversation were such that, on seeing or hearing you, people would say: This man reads the life of Jesus Christ.
quote.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá

2027-12-24.seasonLabel: Friday of the Fourth Week of Advent

quote.text: O human, who are fragile dust of the earth and ashes of ashes! Cry out and speak of the origin of pure salvation until those people are instructed, who, though they see the inmost contents of the Scriptures, do not wish to tell them or preach them, because they are lukewarm and sluggish in serving God's justice.
quote.author: St. Hildegard of Bingen

quote.text: The Christian mysteries are an indivisible whole. The way from Bethlehem leads inevitably to Golgotha, from the crib to the Cross. The star of Bethlehem shines in the night of sin. The shadow of the Cross falls on the light that shines from the crib. This light is extinguished in the darkness of Good Friday, but it rises all the more brilliantly in the sun of grace on the morning of the Resurrection.
quote.author: St. Edith Stein (Teresa Benedicta of the Cross)

2027-12-26.seasonLabel: Second Day within the Octave of Christmas
2027-12-26.celebration: The Holy Family of Jesus, Mary and Joseph

quote.text: To-day Godhead sealed itself upon Manhood, that so with the Godhead's stamp Manhood might be adorned.
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian

2027-12-27.seasonLabel: Third Day within the Octave of Christmas

quote.text: The men who have gone beyond this, giving themselves up to God in the dying spiritual way and by renouncing all things, attaining to a state above the contemplation of the forms and images of the mind—in such men, let me assure you, children, God finds His place of rest; there He dwells and there He works as He wills.
quote.author: Johannes Tauler

2027-12-28.seasonLabel: Fourth Day within the Octave of Christmas

quote.text: The Coming of God to Man, that we might go forth, or rather (for this is the more proper expression) that we might go back to God — that putting off the old man, we might put on the New; and that as we died in Adam, so we might live in Christ, being born with Christ and crucified with Him and buried with Him and rising with Him.
quote.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus

2027-12-29.seasonLabel: Fifth Day within the Octave of Christmas

quote.text: And the Word was made flesh and tabernacled in us (and we saw His glory, the glory as of the Only-Begotten of the Father) full of grace and truth.
quote.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria

2027-12-30.seasonLabel: Sixth Day within the Octave of Christmas

quote.text: Christian, acknowledge thy dignity, and becoming a partner in the Divine nature, refuse to return to the old baseness by degenerate conduct.
quote.author: St. Leo the Great

2027-12-31.seasonLabel: Seventh Day within the Octave of Christmas

quote.text: The Babe that I carry carries me, says Mary, and He has lowered His wings, and taken and placed me between His pinions, and mounted into the air; and a promise has been given me that height and depth shall be my Son's.
quote.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
